This is about Learning API Give me xgboost.train(params, dtrain, num_boost_round=10, *, evals=None, obj=None, feval=None, maximize=None, early_stopping_rounds=None, evals_result=None, verbose_eval=True, xgb_model=None, callbacks=None, custom_metric=None) Full description 8 uses cases in real life Syntax Parameters description (in a non-table format) Return value give me 5 very detailed and good examples (make it long) give the result for each example with a pandas dataframe show me the print() output






Below is a comprehensive guide to the xgboost.train() function (often called the “learning API”) in XGBoost. This function provides a lower-level yet flexible way to train an XGBoost model (i.e., a Booster) using a provided dataset, parameters, and optional evaluation data. Unlike the scikit-learn-like interfaces (XGBRegressor, XGBClassifier, etc.), xgboost.train() gives you direct control over the training loop, callbacks, early stopping, and more.
________________


Full Description
xgboost.train(params, dtrain, num_boost_round=10, *, evals=None, obj=None, feval=None, maximize=None, early_stopping_rounds=None, evals_result=None, verbose_eval=True, xgb_model=None, callbacks=None, custom_metric=None) trains a gradient boosting model using the specified parameters, training data, and optional custom components. Here are some key points:
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     1. Parameters (params) control the objective function (like regression vs. classification), tree-building details (max_depth, eta, etc.), and other advanced features (regularization, GPU usage, etc.).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     2. dtrain is your training data, typically an xgboost.DMatrix containing features/labels, but can be other forms if you rely on internal conversions.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     3. num_boost_round is how many boosting iterations (trees) to build, though early stopping might end earlier.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     4. evals can hold additional datasets (like validation sets) so XGBoost can report metrics or apply early stopping.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     5. Callbacks and custom objectives/metrics (obj, feval, custom_metric) let you deeply customize the training process.

________________


8 Use Cases in Real Life
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        1. Regression on Large Tabular Data: A data scientist trains a regression model for house prices. They use xgboost.train(...) specifying a “reg:squarederror” objective, monitoring a validation set.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        2. Binary Classification with Early Stopping: A machine learning engineer trains a churn model, sets early_stopping_rounds=10, and provides (dtrain, "train"), (dval, "eval") in evals to automatically stop if no improvement.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        3. Multi-Class Classification: A model for image classification with a few classes uses objective="multi:softmax" and num_class=5. The user passes in multiple eval sets to watch performance.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        4. Custom Objective: A finance quant implements a special loss function for risk metrics, hooking it into obj=custom_obj_func. They also supply a custom evaluation metric with feval=custom_eval_func.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        5. GPU Acceleration: A user sets 'tree_method': 'gpu_hist' in params for large dataset speedup, training the model on GPU resources.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        6. Incremental Training: They load a previous model via xgb_model="previous.model", continuing from existing trees for more boosting rounds.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        7. Callback-based Logging: They pass callbacks that do custom logging each iteration, for example printing advanced stats or implementing custom alerts.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        8. Experimental Tuning with Custom Metric: A user tries an advanced ranking metric or a domain-specific metric, specifying it via custom_metric=… or feval=…, analyzing results in evals_result.

________________


Syntax
xgboost.train(
    params,
    dtrain,
    num_boost_round=10,
    *,
    evals=None,
    obj=None,
    feval=None,
    maximize=None,
    early_stopping_rounds=None,
    evals_result=None,
    verbose_eval=True,
    xgb_model=None,
    callbacks=None,
    custom_metric=None
)


(All arguments after num_boost_round=10 are keyword-only in modern XGBoost versions.)
________________


Parameters Description (Non-Table Format)
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           * params:
 A dictionary of booster parameters, e.g. {"objective": "reg:squarederror", "eta": 0.1, "max_depth": 3}. These control the model’s objective, tree-building logic, regularization, GPU usage, etc.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           * dtrain:
 The training dataset, typically a xgboost.DMatrix with features and labels. Alternatively, you can pass arrays/pandas in some XGBoost versions, but usually a DMatrix is more explicit.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           * num_boost_round (int, default=10):
 How many boosting iterations (trees) to build. If early_stopping_rounds is used, the actual number of rounds might be fewer.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           * evals (list of (dmatrix, name) tuples, default=None):
 A list of (DMatrix, "eval_name") pairs for which XGBoost will evaluate metrics at each iteration. For example, [ (dtrain, "train"), (dvalid, "eval") ].

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           * obj (callable, default=None):
 A custom objective function. If None, XGBoost uses the built-in objective from params["objective"].

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           * feval (callable, default=None):
 A custom evaluation metric function, returning (eval_name, result, is_higher_better). If None, uses the metric from params["eval_metric"].

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           * maximize (bool, default=None):
 Whether we want to maximize the metric used for early stopping. If not set, XGBoost tries to guess based on the objective or metric name.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           * early_stopping_rounds (int, default=None):
 If set, stops training if the eval metric has not improved for this many rounds. Usually requires evals with at least one (dmatrix, name).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           * evals_result (dict, default=None):
 If provided, XGBoost fills this dictionary with evaluation results: evals_result[name][metric] = list_of_values_per_iteration.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           * verbose_eval (bool or int, default=True):

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              * True: prints evaluation messages every iteration.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              * False: no printing.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              * int: prints every verbose_eval iterations.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 * xgb_model (str or Booster or None, default=None):
 If set to a path or an existing Booster, XGBoost continues training from that model or reuses it for initialization.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 * callbacks (list of callables, default=None):
 Advanced usage: pass a list of callback functions that get invoked at certain points (like after each iteration). For custom logging, custom stopping, etc.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 * custom_metric (callable, default=None):
 Another way to specify a custom metric, similar to feval. In some versions, feval or custom_metric can be used interchangeably or combined.

________________


Return Value
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    * Return Value:
 A xgboost.Booster object. This Booster is the trained model after num_boost_round boosting iterations (or fewer if early stopping is triggered).

________________


5 Very Detailed Examples of xgboost.train()
Below are five extended scenarios that illustrate how to call xgboost.train() with different parameters. Each example ends with a small pandas DataFrame print demonstrating relevant outcomes.
________________


Example 1: Basic Regression Model with a Validation Set
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd


# 1) Generate synthetic regression data
np.random.seed(42)
X_train = np.random.rand(100, 3)
y_train = X_train[:,0]*2 + X_train[:,1]*-1.5 + np.random.randn(100)*0.1
dtrain = xgb.DMatrix(X_train, label=y_train)


X_val = np.random.rand(20, 3)
y_val = X_val[:,0]*2 + X_val[:,1]*-1.5 + np.random.randn(20)*0.1
dval = xgb.DMatrix(X_val, label=y_val)


# 2) Define parameters
params_reg = {
    "objective": "reg:squarederror",
    "eta": 0.1,
    "max_depth": 3
}
# 3) Train, specifying eval sets
evals = [(dtrain, "train"), (dval, "eval")]
booster_basic = xgb.train(params_reg, dtrain, num_boost_round=10, evals=evals, verbose_eval=False)


# 4) Predictions
preds = booster_basic.predict(dval)


# 5) Show some results
df_ex1 = pd.DataFrame({
    "ValSample": range(5),
    "Pred": preds[:5],
    "Label": y_val[:5]
})
print("Example 1: Basic Regression Model with a Validation Set")
print(df_ex1)


Simulated print() output:
Example 1: Basic Regression Model with a Validation Set
   ValSample      Pred     Label
0          0  2.123400  2.145600
1          1  1.978900  1.896700
2          2  2.234500  2.210100
3          3  1.845600  1.756700
4          4  2.100100  2.089000


________________


Example 2: Binary Classification with Early Stopping
We train a classifier, specify a validation set, and set early_stopping_rounds=5, meaning if the eval metric fails to improve for 5 consecutive rounds, training stops early.
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd


# Synthetic binary classification data
np.random.seed(101)
X_train_cls = np.random.rand(150, 4)
y_train_cls = (X_train_cls[:,0] + X_train_cls[:,1] > 1).astype(int)
dtrain_cls = xgb.DMatrix(X_train_cls, label=y_train_cls)


X_val_cls = np.random.rand(30, 4)
y_val_cls = (X_val_cls[:,0] + X_val_cls[:,1] > 1).astype(int)
dval_cls = xgb.DMatrix(X_val_cls, label=y_val_cls)


params_cls = {
    "objective": "binary:logistic",
    "eval_metric": "logloss",
    "eta": 0.1,
    "max_depth": 3
}
evals_cls = [(dtrain_cls, "train"), (dval_cls, "eval")]


booster_es = xgb.train(
    params_cls,
    dtrain_cls,
    num_boost_round=100,
    evals=evals_cls,
    early_stopping_rounds=5,
    verbose_eval=False
)


df_ex2 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Best Score": [booster_es.best_score],
    "Best Iteration": [booster_es.best_iteration],
    "Num Rounds Used": [booster_es.num_boosted_rounds()]
})
print("\nExample 2: Binary Classification with Early Stopping")
print(df_ex2)


Simulated print() output:
Example 2: Binary Classification with Early Stopping
   Best Score  Best Iteration  Num Rounds Used
0     0.43210              28               29


(We see training might have ended at iteration ~28, so total rounds = 29.)
________________


Example 3: Custom Objective and Custom Evaluation Function
We define a custom objective (squared error variant) and a custom eval function. We pass them via obj and feval.
def custom_obj(preds, dtrain):
    labels = dtrain.get_label()
    grad = preds - labels
    hess = np.ones_like(preds)
    return grad, hess


def custom_eval_func(preds, dtrain):
    labels = dtrain.get_label()
    mse = ((preds - labels)**2).mean()
    return ("custom_mse", mse, False)  # False => lower is better


X_cust = np.random.rand(60,2)
y_cust = X_cust[:,0]*3 + X_cust[:,1]*-2 + np.random.randn(60)*0.1
dtrain_cust = xgb.DMatrix(X_cust, label=y_cust)


booster_cust = xgb.train(
    params={},  # We won't set an internal objective
    dtrain=dtrain_cust,
    num_boost_round=5,
    obj=custom_obj,
    feval=custom_eval_func,
    evals=[(dtrain_cust, "train")],
    verbose_eval=False
)


preds_cust = booster_cust.predict(dtrain_cust)
mse_final = ((preds_cust - y_cust)**2).mean()


df_ex3 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Final MSE": [mse_final],
    "Trees Built": [booster_cust.num_boosted_rounds()]
})
print("\nExample 3: Custom Objective and Custom Evaluation Function")
print(df_ex3)


Simulated print() output:
Example 3: Custom Objective and Custom Evaluation Function
   Final MSE  Trees Built
0   0.034560            5


________________


Example 4: Passing a Previously Trained Model via xgb_model
We train a small model, save it, then do an additional training on top of it by passing the file path or Booster object to xgb_model=.... This merges or continues training.
import os


X4 = np.random.rand(50,3)
y4 = X4[:,0]*1.5 + X4[:,1]*-2 + np.random.randn(50)*0.1
dtrain4 = xgb.DMatrix(X4, label=y4)


# Train initial model
params4 = {"objective": "reg:squarederror", "eta": 0.1, "max_depth": 2}
booster4a = xgb.train(params4, dtrain4, num_boost_round=3)
model_path = "intermediate.model"
booster4a.save_model(model_path)


# Load / continue training
booster4b = xgb.train(params4, dtrain4, num_boost_round=2, xgb_model=model_path)


df_ex4 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Initial Rounds": [3],
    "Additional Rounds": [2],
    "Final Rounds": [booster4b.num_boosted_rounds()],
    "Model File": [model_path]
})
print("\nExample 4: Passing a Previously Trained Model via `xgb_model`")
print(df_ex4)


# Cleanup if needed
if os.path.exists(model_path):
    os.remove(model_path)


Simulated print() output:
Example 4: Passing a Previously Trained Model via `xgb_model`
   Initial Rounds  Additional Rounds  Final Rounds           Model File
0               3                  2             5  intermediate.model


________________


Example 5: Using Callbacks for Custom Logging and Summaries
We can pass a list of callbacks to callbacks=. This example uses xgboost.callback.print_evaluation() with a custom period, and xgboost.callback.record_evaluation() to store logs in a dictionary. We then display that dictionary in a DataFrame.
from xgboost.callback import print_evaluation, record_evaluation


X5 = np.random.rand(100,4)
y5 = (X5[:,0]+X5[:,1]*1.5 + np.random.randn(100)*0.1)
dtrain5 = xgb.DMatrix(X5, label=y5)


params5 = {"objective":"reg:squarederror", "eta":0.05, "max_depth":3}
evals_result5 = {}
cb_print = print_evaluation(period=2)   # Print every 2 rounds
cb_record = record_evaluation(evals_result5)


booster5 = xgb.train(
    params5,
    dtrain5,
    num_boost_round=8,
    evals=[(dtrain5, "train")],
    callbacks=[cb_print, cb_record],
    verbose_eval=False
)


df_eval_res = pd.DataFrame(evals_result5["train"])
df_ex5 = df_eval_res.head(8)
print("\nExample 5: Using Callbacks for Custom Logging and Summaries")
print(df_ex5)


Simulated print() output (some console logs might appear from print_evaluation, but we only show final df):
Example 5: Using Callbacks for Custom Logging and Summaries
    rmse
0  1.234
1  0.978
2  0.845
3  0.778
4  0.734
5  0.706
6  0.698
7  0.689


(Each row is a round index, with a metric column. Real values differ.)

Summary
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       * Function: xgboost.train(params, dtrain, num_boost_round=10, *, evals=None, obj=None, feval=None, maximize=None, early_stopping_rounds=None, evals_result=None, verbose_eval=True, xgb_model=None, callbacks=None, custom_metric=None)

Syntax:

xgboost.train(
    params,
    dtrain,
    num_boost_round=10,
    *,
    evals=None,
    obj=None,
    feval=None,
    maximize=None,
    early_stopping_rounds=None,
    evals_result=None,
    verbose_eval=True,
    xgb_model=None,
    callbacks=None,
    custom_metric=None
)
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          *                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           * Parameters (Non-Table Format):

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             * params (dict): XGBoost parameters (like objective, eta, max_depth, etc.).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             * dtrain (DMatrix): The training dataset.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             * num_boost_round (int): Number of boosting iterations (default=10).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             * evals (list): List of (dmatrix, name) pairs for evaluation metrics.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             * obj (callable): Custom objective function, or None for default.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             * feval (callable): Custom evaluation function, or None for default.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             * maximize (bool): Whether the main metric is to be maximized or minimized.
