This is about Scikit-Learn API Give me get_num_boosting_rounds() Full description Syntax Parameters description (in a non-table format) Return value give me 5 very detailed and good examples (make it long) give the result for each example with a pandas dataframe show me the print() output










Below is a comprehensive guide to a get_num_boosting_rounds() method in XGBoost’s Scikit-Learn API (e.g., in XGBRegressor, XGBClassifier, etc.). Note that this method is not part of older or more common scikit-learn usage—it may be specific to newer versions of XGBoost or internal modifications. In many cases, users rely on n_estimators or the underlying Booster’s method (model.get_booster().num_boosted_rounds()). If your scikit-learn XGBoost model provides get_num_boosting_rounds(), it presumably returns the total number of boosting rounds used in the final trained model (often influenced by early stopping, or merging from a previously trained model).
________________


Full Description
When training an XGBoost model through scikit-learn’s API, you typically specify n_estimators (which is the maximum number of boosting rounds). If you use features like early stopping or incremental training via xgb_model=..., the final model might end up with fewer or more total trees than simply n_estimators. A method called get_num_boosting_rounds() can help you retrieve the actual number of rounds the final model contains, reflecting any partial or additional training.
Key points:
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                1. Early Stopping: If training stopped early, get_num_boosting_rounds() might be less than n_estimators.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                2. Incremental Training: If you resumed from a previous model with X additional rounds, the total might exceed your new n_estimators.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                3. Underlying Booster: Internally, this is typically model.get_booster().num_boosted_rounds(). A scikit-learn style method get_num_boosting_rounds() might simply wrap that.

________________


Syntax
num_rounds = model.get_num_boosting_rounds()


Where model is an instance of XGBRegressor, XGBClassifier, or a similar XGBoost scikit-learn estimator that implements this method.
________________


Parameters Description (Non-Table Format)
There are no parameters for get_num_boosting_rounds(). It simply returns an integer indicating how many boosting rounds (trees) are in the final ensemble. Typically, if the method is not implemented, you might replicate it by calling:
model.get_booster().num_boosted_rounds()


________________


Return Value
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   * Return Value:
An integer representing the total number of boosting rounds in the final model. This might be different from the original n_estimators if you used early stopping or incremental training.

________________


5 Very Detailed Examples of get_num_boosting_rounds()
Below are five scenarios illustrating how you might use or interpret get_num_boosting_rounds(). In each example, we finish with a pandas DataFrame demonstration that includes the reported number of boosting rounds.
________________


Example 1: Basic Regression Without Early Stopping
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd


# 1) Generate synthetic data
np.random.seed(42)
X = np.random.rand(50,3)
y = X[:,0]*2.0 + np.random.randn(50)*0.1


# 2) Train a simple XGBRegressor (no early stopping)
model_reg = xgb.XGBRegressor(n_estimators=5)
model_reg.fit(X, y)


# 3) Suppose method is available:
num_rounds = model_reg.get_num_boosting_rounds()  # or get_booster().num_boosted_rounds()


# 4) Display in a DataFrame
df_ex1 = pd.DataFrame({"n_estimators_requested": [5], 
                       "num_boosting_rounds": [num_rounds]})
print("Example 1: Basic Regression Without Early Stopping")
print(df_ex1)


Simulated print() output:
Example 1: Basic Regression Without Early Stopping
   n_estimators_requested  num_boosting_rounds
0                       5                    5


(We see the final model used all 5 rounds, matching n_estimators.)
________________


Example 2: Binary Classification with Early Stopping
If we enable early stopping, the actual number of rounds might be less than n_estimators. Then get_num_boosting_rounds() returns the truncated count.
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd


X2 = np.random.rand(100,4)
y2 = (X2[:,0]+X2[:,1]>1).astype(int)
model_cls = xgb.XGBClassifier(n_estimators=20)
model_cls.fit(
    X2, y2,
    eval_set=[(X2,y2)],  # just for demonstration
    early_stopping_rounds=5,
    verbose=False
)


num_rounds2 = model_cls.get_num_boosting_rounds()
df_ex2 = pd.DataFrame({
    "n_estimators_requested": [20],
    "num_boosting_rounds": [num_rounds2]
})
print("\nExample 2: Binary Classification with Early Stopping")
print(df_ex2)


Simulated print() output:
Example 2: Binary Classification with Early Stopping
   n_estimators_requested  num_boosting_rounds
0                      20                  13


(We see only 13 rounds used due to no improvement for 5 consecutive iterations.)
________________


Example 3: Merging from a Previous Model (Incremental Training)
If you pass an existing model to xgb_model=..., you might end up with more total rounds than your new n_estimators. Then get_num_boosting_rounds() might reflect the sum of old plus new.
import os


X3 = np.random.rand(40,3)
y3 = X3[:,0]*2.5 + np.random.randn(40)*0.2
model_init = xgb.XGBRegressor(n_estimators=5)
model_init.fit(X3, y3)


model_file = "temp_model.json"
model_init.save_model(model_file)


model_cont = xgb.XGBRegressor(n_estimators=3)
model_cont.fit(X3, y3, xgb_model=model_file, verbose=False)


num_rounds3 = model_cont.get_num_boosting_rounds()
df_ex3 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Initial_n_estimators": [5],
    "Continuing_n_estimators": [3],
    "Final_Num_Boosting_Rounds": [num_rounds3]
})
print("\nExample 3: Merging from a Previous Model (Incremental Training)")
print(df_ex3)


if os.path.exists(model_file):
    os.remove(model_file)


Simulated print() output:
Example 3: Merging from a Previous Model (Incremental Training)
   Initial_n_estimators  Continuing_n_estimators  Final_Num_Boosting_Rounds
0                     5                         3                         8


(We see 8 total rounds, presumably 5 from the old model plus 3 new ones.)
________________


Example 4: Multi-Class Classification Confirming Total Rounds
For multi-class tasks, get_num_boosting_rounds() still returns the total number of boosting rounds. Each round might build multiple trees (one per class in softmax), but XGBoost counts them collectively as “one iteration per class.” Let’s demonstrate:
X4 = np.random.rand(60,5)
y4 = np.random.randint(0,3,size=60)  # 3 classes


model_mc = xgb.XGBClassifier(n_estimators=10, objective="multi:softprob", num_class=3)
model_mc.fit(X4, y4)


rounds4 = model_mc.get_num_boosting_rounds()
df_ex4 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Requested": [10],
    "Used": [rounds4]
})
print("\nExample 4: Multi-Class Classification Confirming Total Rounds")
print(df_ex4)


Simulated print() output:
Example 4: Multi-Class Classification Confirming Total Rounds
   Requested  Used
0         10    10


(We see it used all 10 boosting rounds, but internally that’s 10 * 3 = 30 trees for 3 classes.)
________________


Example 5: Checking Rounds in a scikit-learn Pipeline
If you put an XGB model in a scikit-learn Pipeline, you can still call get_num_boosting_rounds() on the final step. This demonstrates how it might integrate into a more complex workflow.
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler


X5 = np.random.rand(50,2)
y5 = X5[:,0]*1.5 + np.random.randn(50)*0.2


pipeline5 = Pipeline([
    ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
    ("xgb", xgb.XGBRegressor(n_estimators=7))
])
pipeline5.fit(X5, y5)


xgb_step = pipeline5.named_steps["xgb"]
rounds5 = xgb_step.get_num_boosting_rounds()


df_ex5 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Pipeline_n_estimators": [7],
    "Final_Boosting_Rounds": [rounds5]
})
print("\nExample 5: Checking Rounds in a scikit-learn Pipeline")
print(df_ex5)


Simulated print() output:
Example 5: Checking Rounds in a scikit-learn Pipeline
   Pipeline_n_estimators  Final_Boosting_Rounds
0                      7                     7


________________


Summary
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      * Method: get_num_boosting_rounds() in XGBoost scikit-learn estimators

Syntax:

rounds = model.get_num_boosting_rounds()
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         *                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          * Parameters:
(None) – it’s a no-parameter method returning an integer.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         * Return Value:
An integer representing the final total number of boosting rounds in the model. This can differ from n_estimators if early stopping or incremental training changed the final ensemble size.

The five examples demonstrate typical usage—(1) basic regression with no early stopping, (2) classification with early stopping, (3) incremental training, (4) multi-class classification, and (5) usage in a scikit-learn pipeline. We show final results in a pandas DataFrame for clarity.
