This is about Scikit-Learn API Give me get_params(deep=True) Full description Syntax Parameters description (in a non-table format) Return value give me 5 very detailed and good examples (make it long) give the result for each example with a pandas dataframe show me the print() output








Below is a comprehensive guide to the get_params(deep=True) method in the Scikit-Learn API. This method is part of the scikit-learn estimator interface, including XGBoost’s scikit-learn wrappers (e.g., XGBRegressor, XGBClassifier). It returns a dictionary of all parameters of the estimator, optionally including parameters of sub-estimators or sub-objects if deep=True.
While this is a standard scikit-learn method, we illustrate usage in the context of XGBoost’s scikit-learn estimators. The details are consistent with scikit-learn convention across many estimators.
________________


Full Description
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            1. Purpose

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               * get_params(deep=True) is used to retrieve all the public parameters of an estimator in a dictionary form. If deep=True, it recursively fetches the parameters of contained sub-estimators or objects.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               * For XGBoost scikit-learn estimators (e.g., XGBRegressor), this will list all constructor parameters like n_estimators, max_depth, learning_rate, plus anything inherited from scikit-learn base classes (such as n_jobs, random_state).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  2. Typical Usage

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     * Debugging or introspection: You can see what parameters your model is actually using after creation.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     * Pipelines: In a pipeline or param grid, scikit-learn calls get_params() to see what can be tuned with GridSearchCV or RandomizedSearchCV.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     * Copying or reconstructing: You might retrieve params from one estimator to create a new instance with the same settings.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        3. Relation to set_params(...)

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           * Typically, scikit-learn allows you to do model.set_params(**some_params) to update parameters, and you can see them by model.get_params(deep=True).

________________


Syntax
param_dict = estimator.get_params(deep=True)


Where estimator is typically an XGBRegressor, XGBClassifier, or another scikit-learn style model.
________________


Parameters Description (Non-Table Format)
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              * deep (bool, default=True):
If True, recursively fetches parameters from sub-objects (like sub-estimators, transformers, etc.). If False, only returns parameters of the top-level object.

(No other parameters are accepted. deep is the only one.)
________________


Return Value
Return Value:
A dictionary mapping "parameter_name" to value. The parameter names are the public constructor arguments of the estimator and sub-estimator arguments if deep=True. For example:

{
  "n_estimators": 100,
  "max_depth": 6,
  "learning_rate": 0.3,
  "random_state": 42,
  ...
}
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 * The exact keys vary depending on the estimator and version of XGBoost.
________________


5 Very Detailed Examples of get_params(deep=True)
Below are five examples demonstrating how you might call get_params(deep=True) on scikit-learn–compatible XGBoost models and interpret the dictionary that is returned. Each example ends with a pandas DataFrame illustration for clarity.
________________


Example 1: Basic Usage on XGBRegressor
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd


# 1) Generate synthetic data
X1 = np.random.rand(10, 3)
y1 = X1[:,0]*2.5 - 1.2*X1[:,1] + np.random.randn(10)*0.1


# 2) Create an XGBRegressor
model_reg = xgb.XGBRegressor(n_estimators=5, max_depth=2, random_state=42)
model_reg.fit(X1, y1)


# 3) Call get_params(deep=True)
params_dict = model_reg.get_params(deep=True)


# 4) Convert partial results to a DataFrame for display
df_ex1 = pd.DataFrame(
    list(params_dict.items())[:7],  # just show a subset
    columns=["ParamName","Value"]
)
print("Example 1: Basic Usage on `XGBRegressor`")
print(df_ex1)


Simulated print() output:
Example 1: Basic Usage on `XGBRegressor`
            ParamName               Value
0         objective           reg:squarederror
1        n_estimators                        5
2           max_depth                        2
3        random_state                       42
4        learning_rate                      0.3
5  booster                gbtree
6        tree_method                auto


________________


Example 2: XGBClassifier with Additional Parameters
If you specify more advanced parameters (like subsample=0.8, colsample_bytree=0.9, or eval_metric='error'), get_params() includes them.
X2 = np.random.rand(15,4)
y2 = (X2[:,0]+X2[:,1]>1).astype(int)


model_cls = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    n_estimators=10,
    max_depth=3,
    eval_metric="error",
    subsample=0.8,
    colsample_bytree=0.9,
    use_label_encoder=False
)
model_cls.fit(X2, y2)


params_dict2 = model_cls.get_params(deep=True)


df_ex2 = pd.DataFrame(
    list(params_dict2.items())[:8],  # show subset
    columns=["Param","Value"]
)
print("\nExample 2: `XGBClassifier` with Additional Parameters")
print(df_ex2)


Simulated print() output:
Example 2: `XGBClassifier` with Additional Parameters
                Param       Value
0        objective  binary:logistic
1     n_estimators            10
2        max_depth             3
3      eval_metric         error
4        subsample           0.8
5  colsample_bytree           0.9
6  use_label_encoder         False
7     random_state             0


________________


Example 3: Setting deep=False to Limit Output
We can call get_params(deep=False) to only get top-level params, ignoring sub-estimator details (like _Booster or internal components). Often scikit-learn sets deep=True by default, but you can see the difference:
X3 = np.random.rand(12,2)
y3 = X3[:,0]*1.5 + np.random.randn(12)*0.1
model_reg3 = xgb.XGBRegressor(n_estimators=3, booster="gbtree")
model_reg3.fit(X3, y3)


params_deep_true = model_reg3.get_params(deep=True)
params_deep_false = model_reg3.get_params(deep=False)


df_ex3 = pd.DataFrame({
    "deep=True params count": [len(params_deep_true)],
    "deep=False params count": [len(params_deep_false)]
})
print("\nExample 3: Setting `deep=False` to Limit Output")
print(df_ex3)


Simulated print() output:
Example 3: Setting `deep=False` to Limit Output
   deep=True params count  deep=False params count
0                       39                         26


(We see more keys when deep=True because it includes sub-objects. The actual numbers are just examples.)
________________


Example 4: Retrieving Parameters in a Pipeline Context
If you have a scikit-learn Pipeline with an XGB model as the final step, calling pipeline.get_params(deep=True) merges pipeline steps plus the XGB estimator’s parameters. Here’s how you might see partial results.
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler


X4 = np.random.rand(10,3)
y4 = X4[:,0]*2.2 + np.random.randn(10)*0.1


pipeline4 = Pipeline([
    ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
    ("xgb", xgb.XGBRegressor(n_estimators=5, learning_rate=0.1))
])
pipeline4.fit(X4, y4)


params_p4 = pipeline4.get_params(deep=True)


df_ex4 = pd.DataFrame(
    list(params_p4.items())[:8],
    columns=["Key","Value"]
)
print("\nExample 4: Retrieving Parameters in a Pipeline Context")
print(df_ex4)


Simulated print() output:
Example 4: Retrieving Parameters in a Pipeline Context
                            Key             Value
0                   memory                None
1  steps                         [('scaler', StandardScaler()), ('xgb', XGBReg...
2      scaler                      StandardScaler()
3  scaler__copy                        True
4  scaler__with_mean                  True
5  scaler__with_std                   True
6        xgb       XGBRegressor(base_score=0.5, booster='gbtree', ...
7  xgb__n_estimators                  5


(We see pipeline parameters plus the XGBRegressor’s parameters. The full dictionary can be large.)
________________


Example 5: Using get_params() for Hyperparameter Searching or Cloning
Scikit-learn’s GridSearchCV and clone rely on get_params() to see what can be set. If you want to do a manual approach, you can do:
X5 = np.random.rand(25,3)
y5 = X5[:,0]*2.0 + np.random.randn(25)*0.1


model5 = xgb.XGBRegressor(n_estimators=10, max_depth=4, learning_rate=0.05)
model5_params = model5.get_params(deep=True)


# Suppose we want to try a new model with slightly different max_depth
new_params = dict(model5_params)
new_params["max_depth"] = 6
model5_alt = type(model5)(**new_params)  # construct a new instance with updated param


df_ex5 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Original max_depth": [model5_params["max_depth"]],
    "New max_depth": [new_params["max_depth"]]
})
print("\nExample 5: Using `get_params()` for Hyperparameter Searching or Cloning")
print(df_ex5)


Simulated print() output:
Example 5: Using `get_params()` for Hyperparameter Searching or Cloning
   Original max_depth  New max_depth
0                   4              6


________________


Summary
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 * Method: get_params(deep=True) in scikit-learn XGBoost estimators (XGBRegressor, XGBClassifier, etc.).

Syntax:

param_dict = model.get_params(deep=True)
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    *                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     * Parameters:

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       * deep (bool, default=True): If True, recursively fetch sub-object parameters. If False, only top-level.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          * Return Value:
A dictionary mapping parameter names to current values for the estimator (and sub-estimators if deep=True). This is a standard scikit-learn feature, used for introspection and hyperparameter searching.

The five examples show typical usage: (1) basic usage on a regression model, (2) classification with extra parameters, (3) limiting output by setting deep=False, (4) retrieving parameters in a pipeline, and (5) usage for manual hyperparameter searching or cloning. Each scenario ends with a small pandas DataFrame to illustrate the relevant portion of the resulting dictionary in the print output.




























































































































































This is about Scikit-Learn API Give me get_xgb_params() Full description Syntax Parameters description (in a non-table format) Return value give me 5 very detailed and good examples (make it long) give the result for each example with a pandas dataframe show me the print() output












Below is a comprehensive guide to the get_xgb_params() method in XGBoost’s Scikit-Learn API (such as XGBRegressor, XGBClassifier, etc.). Unlike get_params(), which returns all scikit-learn‐style parameters (including things like n_jobs, random_state, etc.), get_xgb_params() focuses on the subset of parameters that are specifically relevant to the underlying XGBoost Booster. In other words, it extracts just the “XGBoost-side” parameters (like max_depth, learning_rate, subsample, etc.) in a dictionary format.
________________


Full Description
When you instantiate and train a scikit-learn XGBoost model (e.g., XGBRegressor), the model typically stores parameters that come from both scikit-learn conventions (n_estimators, random_state, verbosity, etc.) and from the native XGBoost booster (like max_depth, eta, gamma). In many cases, some scikit-learn parameters act as a convenience layer around XGBoost parameters, or they are purely scikit-learn features (like n_jobs). The get_xgb_params() method filters out those scikit-learn–only arguments and returns the dictionary of XGBoost booster parameters that the model actually uses under the hood.
Key points:
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             1. Focus on XGBoost Booster: The returned dictionary typically includes parameters such as "objective", "booster", "max_depth", "learning_rate" (alias of eta), "subsample", "colsample_bytree", etc.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             2. No scikit-learn–only parameters: It excludes scikit-learn–specific parameters (like n_estimators) or pipeline features (like steps in a pipeline).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             3. Used for advanced debugging or integration: You might call get_xgb_params() if you want to see exactly which XGBoost parameters were set, especially if bridging to the native XGBoost Python API or the Booster.

________________


Syntax
xgb_params_dict = model.get_xgb_params()


Where model is typically an instance of XGBRegressor, XGBClassifier, or another scikit-learn style XGBoost estimator that has been initialized and possibly fitted.
________________


Parameters Description (Non-Table Format)
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                * No parameters:
get_xgb_params() is a no-argument method. It returns a dictionary mapping parameter names (strings) to their current values.

________________


Return Value
Return Value:
A dictionary of XGBoost booster parameters that the scikit-learn model uses internally. For example, something like:

{
    "objective": "reg:squarederror",
    "booster": "gbtree",
    "max_depth": 6,
    "learning_rate": 0.3,
    "gamma": 0,
    "min_child_weight": 1,
    "subsample": 1,
    ...
}
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   * (Exactly which keys are present depends on your version of XGBoost and how you configured the model.)
________________


5 Very Detailed Examples of get_xgb_params()
Below are five scenarios illustrating how to call get_xgb_params() on scikit-learn style XGBoost models and interpret the results. Each example ends with a pandas DataFrame illustration for clarity.
________________


Example 1: Basic XGBRegressor with Default Parameters
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd


# 1) Generate synthetic data
np.random.seed(42)
X = np.random.rand(50, 3)
y = X[:,0]*2.0 - 1.0*X[:,1] + np.random.randn(50)*0.1


# 2) Create an XGBRegressor with mostly defaults
model_reg = xgb.XGBRegressor(n_estimators=5, random_state=42)
model_reg.fit(X, y)


# 3) Retrieve XGBoost-specific parameters
xgb_params = model_reg.get_xgb_params()


# 4) Convert partial results to DataFrame
df_ex1 = pd.DataFrame(list(xgb_params.items()), columns=["Key","Value"])
df_ex1 = df_ex1.head(8)  # show a subset
print("Example 1: Basic XGBRegressor with Default Parameters")
print(df_ex1)


Simulated print() output:
Example 1: Basic XGBRegressor with Default Parameters
                   Key               Value
0           objective    reg:squarederror
1             booster             gbtree
2          learning_rate               0.3
3             max_depth                 6
4                 eta                 0.3
5               gamma                   0
6   min_child_weight                   1
7           subsample                   1


(We see standard XGBoost booster params, like "objective": "reg:squarederror", "learning_rate": 0.3", "max_depth": 6", etc.)
________________


Example 2: Classification with Additional Booster Parameters
Here, we pass some advanced booster parameters (subsample, colsample_bytree, etc.). After fitting, get_xgb_params() shows only the XGBoost-side parameters.
X2 = np.random.rand(60,4)
y2 = (X2[:,0]+X2[:,1]>1).astype(int)


model_cls = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    n_estimators=10,
    max_depth=3,
    subsample=0.8,
    colsample_bytree=0.9,
    learning_rate=0.05,
    use_label_encoder=False
)
model_cls.fit(X2, y2)


params_dict2 = model_cls.get_xgb_params()


df_ex2 = pd.DataFrame(list(params_dict2.items()), columns=["ParamName","Value"])
df_ex2 = df_ex2.sort_values(by="ParamName")
print("\nExample 2: Classification with Additional Booster Parameters")
print(df_ex2.head(10))


Simulated print() output:
Example 2: Classification with Additional Booster Parameters
         ParamName                 Value
6     booster                   gbtree
3   colsample_bynode               1
4   colsample_bylevel              1
5   colsample_bytree             0.9
1         eta                    0.05
7       gamma                      0
2  learning_rate                0.05
8   max_delta_step                 0
9   max_depth                      3
0   objective         binary:logistic


________________


Example 3: Demonstrating That scikit-learn–only Params Are Excluded
Parameters like n_estimators, random_state, or verbosity are typically scikit-learn–side. They do not show up in get_xgb_params(). We illustrate by verifying they are missing.
X3 = np.random.rand(10,2)
y3 = np.random.rand(10)


model_reg3 = xgb.XGBRegressor(
    n_estimators=15,
    verbosity=2,   # scikit-learn param
    random_state=999,
    max_depth=4
)
model_reg3.fit(X3, y3)


params_dict3 = model_reg3.get_xgb_params()


# We'll check whether 'n_estimators' or 'verbosity' are in dict
df_ex3 = pd.DataFrame(
    {
     "Contains_n_estimators": ["n_estimators" in params_dict3],
     "Contains_verbosity": ["verbosity" in params_dict3],
     "Contains_max_depth": ["max_depth" in params_dict3]
    }
)
print("\nExample 3: Demonstrating That scikit-learn–only Params Are Excluded")
print(df_ex3)


Simulated print() output:
Example 3: Demonstrating That scikit-learn–only Params Are Excluded
   Contains_n_estimators  Contains_verbosity  Contains_max_depth
0                  False               False                True


(We see max_depth is in XGBoost’s param dict, but n_estimators and verbosity are not.)
________________


Example 4: Changing Some Booster Params, Then Checking
We can set booster parameters via the scikit-learn constructor or set_params(), then call get_xgb_params() to see them. Suppose we set "gamma": 5, "min_child_weight": 2, etc.
X4 = np.random.rand(12,3)
y4 = X4[:,0]*3.0 + X4[:,1]*-2.0 + np.random.randn(12)*0.1


model_reg4 = xgb.XGBRegressor()
model_reg4.set_params(  # scikit-learn approach
    gamma=5,
    min_child_weight=2,
    booster="gbtree",
    learning_rate=0.2
)
model_reg4.fit(X4, y4)


dict4 = model_reg4.get_xgb_params()
df_ex4 = pd.DataFrame(list(dict4.items()), columns=["Key","Value"])
df_ex4 = df_ex4.sort_values("Key")
print("\nExample 4: Changing Some Booster Params, Then Checking")
print(df_ex4.head(8))


Simulated print() output:
Example 4: Changing Some Booster Params, Then Checking
               Key      Value
4           booster     gbtree
1               eta        0.2
3           gamma          5
2  learning_rate           0.2
7       max_depth           6
6 max_delta_step           0
5 min_child_weight         2
0      objective reg:squarederror


________________


Example 5: Using get_xgb_params() in a Pipeline for a Multi-Class Classifier
We place an XGBClassifier in a pipeline, set some XGBoost parameters, fit it, then retrieve the final XGBoost booster parameters from the pipeline’s final step.
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler


X5 = np.random.rand(30,4)
y5 = np.random.randint(0,3,size=30)


pipeline5 = Pipeline([
    ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
    ("xgb", xgb.XGBClassifier(
        booster="gbtree",
        num_class=3,
        objective="multi:softprob",
        max_depth=4,
        learning_rate=0.1
    ))
])
pipeline5.fit(X5, y5)


model_xgb_step = pipeline5.named_steps["xgb"]
xgb_params5 = model_xgb_step.get_xgb_params()


df_ex5 = pd.DataFrame(list(xgb_params5.items()), columns=["Param","Value"])
print("\nExample 5: Using `get_xgb_params()` in a Pipeline for a Multi-Class Classifier")
print(df_ex5.head(10))


Simulated print() output:
Example 5: Using `get_xgb_params()` in a Pipeline for a Multi-Class Classifier
              Param                  Value
5  booster                 gbtree
3  learning_rate          0.1
1  eta                    0.1
9  max_depth              4
7  min_child_weight       1
4  gamma                  0
6  max_delta_step         0
8  colsample_bytree       1
2  objective             multi:softprob
0  num_class             3


________________

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   * Method: get_xgb_params() in the scikit-learn XGBoost estimators (e.g., XGBRegressor, XGBClassifier).

Syntax:

xgb_params = model.get_xgb_params()
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      *                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       * Parameters: (None)
 This method does not take arguments.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      * Return Value:
A dictionary of the XGBoost booster parameters used by the model, excluding scikit-learn–only parameters like n_estimators or random_state. Keys might include "objective", "booster", "learning_rate", "max_depth", "gamma", "subsample", etc.

The five examples illustrate typical usage, from basic regression/classification checks, demonstrating that scikit-learn–only parameters are omitted, adjusting XGBoost parameters mid‐flight, and usage in a pipeline context for multi-class. We display partial results with a pandas DataFrame or direct prints of the dictionary.
