Below is a comprehensive guide to the intercept_ property in XGBoost’s Scikit-Learn API. This property is available for estimators that use a linear booster (i.e. when you set booster="gblinear"). In such cases, the model learns a linear function of the input features and, just like in other linear models (for example, scikit‑learn’s LinearRegression), an intercept (or bias) is computed. The intercept_ property provides you with the learned bias term(s) as a NumPy array.
________________


Full Description
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         * Purpose:
 The intercept_ property contains the bias term(s) of the model learned during training when using the linear booster (gblinear). It is analogous to the intercept_ attribute in classic linear models (e.g., LinearRegression or LogisticRegression in scikit‑learn). For a single-output regression model, the property returns an array of shape (1,) (or a scalar value wrapped in an array), while for multi-class classification (when using gblinear), it returns an array with shape (n_classes,).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         * When It’s Available:

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            * Linear Booster Only: This property is only populated when the estimator is constructed with booster="gblinear". For tree-based boosters (gbtree or dart), the model uses an ensemble of decision trees, and an overall intercept is not defined.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            * After Fitting: The property is set after you call .fit(X, y) on the estimator.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               * Usage:
 You can inspect intercept_ to understand the baseline prediction of your model before contributions from the features are added. It is useful for model interpretation, debugging, or for comparison with other linear models.

________________


Syntax
# For a fitted estimator, simply access:
bias = model.intercept_


________________


Parameters Description (Non‑Table Format)
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  * No parameters:
 The property intercept_ does not accept any arguments. It is a read-only attribute available after the model has been fitted.

________________


Return Value
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     * Return Value:
 A NumPy array (ndarray) containing the learned intercept(s):

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        * For single-output regression or binary classification (using gblinear), the shape is typically (1,).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        * For multi-class classification, the shape is (n_classes,).

________________


5 Detailed Examples of intercept_
Below are five detailed examples demonstrating how to use and inspect the intercept_ property. Each example is complete with a code snippet and a simulated printout of the result shown in a pandas DataFrame.
________________


Example 1: Basic Regression with gblinear Booster
In this example, we fit an XGBRegressor using booster="gblinear" on synthetic regression data and then inspect the intercept.
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd


# Generate synthetic regression data
np.random.seed(42)
X_reg = np.random.rand(50, 3)
# Create a linear target with noise
y_reg = 2.0 * X_reg[:, 0] - 1.0 * X_reg[:, 1] + 0.5 * X_reg[:, 2] + np.random.randn(50) * 0.1


# Create and fit XGBRegressor with a linear booster
model_reg = xgb.XGBRegressor(
    booster="gblinear",
    n_estimators=30,
    learning_rate=0.1,
    random_state=42
)
model_reg.fit(X_reg, y_reg)


# Retrieve the intercept
intercept_reg = model_reg.intercept_


# Create a DataFrame to display the intercept
df_ex1 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Parameter": ["Intercept"],
    "Value": intercept_reg
})
print("Example 1: Basic Regression with gblinear Booster")
print(df_ex1)


Simulated print() output:
Example 1: Basic Regression with gblinear Booster
   Parameter     Value
0  Intercept  0.012345


________________


Example 2: Binary Classification with gblinear Booster
For binary classification, we use an XGBClassifier with booster="gblinear". After training, we inspect the intercept, which represents the bias term for the single decision boundary.
# Generate synthetic binary classification data
np.random.seed(101)
X_cls = np.random.rand(100, 4)
y_cls = (X_cls[:, 0] + X_cls[:, 1] > 1).astype(int)


# Create and fit XGBClassifier with linear booster
model_cls = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    booster="gblinear",
    n_estimators=20,
    learning_rate=0.1,
    use_label_encoder=False,
    random_state=101
)
model_cls.fit(X_cls, y_cls)


# Retrieve the intercept
intercept_cls = model_cls.intercept_


# Display in a DataFrame
df_ex2 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Parameter": ["Intercept"],
    "Value": intercept_cls
})
print("\nExample 2: Binary Classification with gblinear Booster")
print(df_ex2)


Simulated print() output:
Example 2: Binary Classification with gblinear Booster
   Parameter     Value
0  Intercept  -0.045678


________________


Example 3: Multi-Class Classification with gblinear Booster
For multi-class classification (e.g., with 3 classes), the XGBClassifier with booster="gblinear" learns one intercept per class.
# Generate synthetic multi-class data
np.random.seed(202)
X_mc = np.random.rand(150, 5)
y_mc = np.random.randint(0, 3, size=150)  # 3 classes


# Create and fit XGBClassifier with gblinear booster for multi-class classification
model_mc = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    booster="gblinear",
    n_estimators=15,
    learning_rate=0.1,
    objective="multi:softprob",
    num_class=3,
    random_state=202,
    use_label_encoder=False
)
model_mc.fit(X_mc, y_mc)


# Retrieve the intercept; expected shape: (3,)
intercept_mc = model_mc.intercept_


# Convert to DataFrame for display
df_ex3 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Class": ["Class 0", "Class 1", "Class 2"],
    "Intercept": intercept_mc
})
print("\nExample 3: Multi-Class Classification with gblinear Booster")
print(df_ex3)


Simulated print() output:
Example 3: Multi-Class Classification with gblinear Booster
     Class  Intercept
0  Class 0    0.123456
1  Class 1   -0.098765
2  Class 2    0.045678


________________


Example 4: Using gblinear in a Pipeline
In this example, we incorporate an XGBRegressor with booster="gblinear" into a scikit-learn pipeline along with a standard scaler. After fitting, we retrieve the intercept from the final estimator.
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler


# Create synthetic data
X_pipe = np.random.rand(50, 2)
y_pipe = X_pipe[:, 0] * 3.0 + np.random.randn(50) * 0.2


# Build a pipeline with StandardScaler and XGBRegressor using gblinear
pipeline = Pipeline([
    ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
    ("xgb", xgb.XGBRegressor(booster="gblinear", n_estimators=10, learning_rate=0.1, random_state=42))
])
pipeline.fit(X_pipe, y_pipe)


# Retrieve intercept from the XGBRegressor in the pipeline
intercept_pipe = pipeline.named_steps["xgb"].intercept_


df_ex4 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Parameter": ["Intercept"],
    "Value": intercept_pipe
})
print("\nExample 4: Using gblinear in a Pipeline")
print(df_ex4)


Simulated print() output:
Example 4: Using gblinear in a Pipeline
   Parameter     Value
0  Intercept  0.034567


________________


Example 5: Comparing Intercepts with Different Regularization Settings
Regularization parameters (such as L1 via alpha and L2 via reg_lambda) can affect the learned intercept. Here, we train two XGBRegressor models with different regularization strengths using booster="gblinear" and compare their intercepts.
# Generate synthetic regression data
np.random.seed(303)
X_reg2 = np.random.rand(60, 3)
y_reg2 = 2.0 * X_reg2[:, 0] - 1.5 * X_reg2[:, 1] + 0.5 * X_reg2[:, 2] + np.random.randn(60)*0.1


# Model with no regularization (default)
model_no_reg = xgb.XGBRegressor(booster="gblinear", n_estimators=20, learning_rate=0.1, random_state=303)
model_no_reg.fit(X_reg2, y_reg2)
intercept_no_reg = model_no_reg.intercept_


# Model with strong regularization
model_reg = xgb.XGBRegressor(
    booster="gblinear",
    n_estimators=20,
    learning_rate=0.1,
    alpha=1.0,         # L1 regularization
    reg_lambda=1.0,    # L2 regularization
    random_state=303
)
model_reg.fit(X_reg2, y_reg2)
intercept_reg = model_reg.intercept_


df_ex5 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Model": ["No Regularization", "Strong Regularization"],
    "Intercept": [intercept_no_reg[0], intercept_reg[0]]
})
print("\nExample 5: Comparing Intercepts with Different Regularization Settings")
print(df_ex5)


Simulated print() output:
Example 5: Comparing Intercepts with Different Regularization Settings
                  Model  Intercept
0     No Regularization    0.012345
1  Strong Regularization   -0.034567


________________


Summary
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           * Property: intercept_ : ndarray

Syntax:

intercept_values = model.intercept_
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              *                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               * Parameters:
None. It is a read-only property set after the model is fitted. (Note: It is available only when using a linear booster, i.e. booster="gblinear". For tree-based boosters, this property does not exist.)

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              * Return Value:
A NumPy array containing the learned intercept (bias) term(s) of the linear model. For single-output regression or binary classification, this is typically an array of shape (1,); for multi-class classification, an array of shape (n_classes,).

The five detailed examples demonstrate:
