This is about Scikit-Learn API classxgboost.XGBClassifier Give me apply(X, iteration_range=None) Full description Syntax Parameters description (in a non-table format) Return value give me 5 very detailed and good examples (make it long) give the result for each example with a pandas dataframe show me the print() output








Below is a comprehensive guide to the apply(X, iteration_range=None) method for the scikit‑learn API class xgboost.XGBClassifier. This method runs each sample in the input XXX through the model’s decision trees and returns the index of the leaf node that each sample falls into for every tree (or a subset of trees if you specify an iteration range). It is especially useful for understanding how the model partitions the feature space or for using the leaf indices as new features in downstream tasks.
________________


Full Description
When you call the apply(X, iteration_range=None) method on a fitted XGBClassifier that uses a tree-based booster (by default, gbtree or dart), the model traverses every tree (or only the trees specified by iteration_range) in the ensemble. For each input sample, it records the index of the leaf node reached in that tree. The output is a NumPy array where:
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       * Each row corresponds to one sample in XXX.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       * Each column corresponds to one boosting round (i.e. one tree, or one group of trees in the case of multi-class classification).

In multi-class classification using a tree booster, the model internally builds one tree per class per boosting iteration (for objectives like "multi:softprob"). However, the scikit‑learn API method apply() returns a 2D array whose interpretation depends on the estimator’s internal design—typically, the returned array contains the leaf indices aggregated over the boosting rounds (or sometimes arranged per class, depending on version and configuration). In many cases, users employ apply() to extract the “leaf index features” for use in feature engineering or subsequent modeling steps.
Key aspects:
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          * Iteration Range:
 If iteration_range is not None, it should be a tuple (start, end), and only the trees from boosting iteration start (inclusive) to end (exclusive) are used for computing the leaf indices.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          * Output for Classification:
 For binary classification, the output is typically a 2D array with shape (n_samples,n_trees)(n\_samples, n\_trees)(n_samples,n_trees). For multi-class classification, the arrangement can vary—often the array has shape (n_samples,n_trees)(n\_samples, n\_trees)(n_samples,n_trees) where each tree’s leaf index is reported in sequence, though sometimes the output is organized in blocks corresponding to each class.

________________


Syntax
leaf_indices = model.apply(X, iteration_range=None)


                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             * model: An instance of xgboost.XGBClassifier that has been fitted.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             * X: The input data (array-like) with shape (n_samples,n_features)(n\_samples, n\_features)(n_samples,n_features).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             * iteration_range: Optional; a tuple (start, end) specifying the boosting rounds to use. If None, all trees are used.

________________


Parameters Description (Non‑Table Format)
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                * X:
The array-like input data for which you want to obtain the leaf indices. It must have the same number of features as the training data.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                * iteration_range (tuple, default=None):
An optional tuple (start, end) that specifies the range of boosting iterations (or trees) to use when applying the model.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   * If set to (0, k), only the first kkk boosting rounds (trees) are used.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   * If None, the method uses all available trees in the ensemble.

________________


Return Value
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      * Return Value:
A NumPy array of shape (n_samples,n_trees)(n\_samples, n\_trees)(n_samples,n_trees) where each element is an integer representing the index of the leaf node reached by the corresponding sample in the corresponding tree.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         * For binary classification, this array is typically two-dimensional with each column corresponding to one tree.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         * For multi-class classification, the shape may reflect the aggregated tree structure (often still a 2D array with n_treesn\_treesn_trees columns, where n_treesn\_treesn_trees is the total number of trees across all boosting rounds).

________________


5 Detailed Examples of apply(X, iteration_range=None)
Below are five detailed examples that illustrate various scenarios using the apply() method on an XGBClassifier. Each example includes a code snippet, an explanation, and a simulated printout of a pandas DataFrame summarizing key outputs.
________________


Example 1: Basic Application with All Trees
In this example, we train an XGBClassifier on synthetic binary classification data and then use apply() to retrieve the leaf indices for each sample using all trees in the ensemble.
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split


# Generate synthetic binary classification data
np.random.seed(42)
X = np.random.rand(150, 4)
y = (X[:, 0] + X[:, 1] > 1).astype(int)


# Split data into training and testing sets
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.3, random_state=42)


# Create and train an XGBClassifier
model = xgb.XGBClassifier(n_estimators=10, max_depth=3, use_label_encoder=False, random_state=42)
model.fit(X_train, y_train, eval_metric="logloss")


# Apply the model to X_test to get leaf indices using all trees
leaf_indices = model.apply(X_test)


# Convert the first 10 rows to a DataFrame for display
df_ex1 = pd.DataFrame(leaf_indices[:10],
                      columns=[f"Tree_{i}" for i in range(leaf_indices.shape[1])])
df_ex1.insert(0, "Sample_Index", np.arange(10))
print("Example 1: Basic Application with All Trees")
print(df_ex1)


Simulated print() output:
Example 1: Basic Application with All Trees
   Sample_Index  Tree_0  Tree_1  Tree_2  Tree_3  Tree_4  Tree_5  Tree_6  Tree_7  Tree_8  Tree_9
0             0       2       3       1       0       2       3       1       0       2       1
1             1       3       2       0       1       3       2       0       1       3       2
2             2       2       3       1       0       2       3       1       0       2       1
3             3       1       0       2       3       1       0       2       3       1       0
4             4       3       2       0       1       3       2       0       1       3       2
5             5       2       1       3       0       2       1       3       0       2       1
6             6       1       0       2       3       1       0       2       3       1       0
7             7       3       2       0       1       3       2       0       1       3       2
8             8       2       3       1       0       2       3       1       0       2       1
9             9       1       0       2       3       1       0       2       3       1       0


________________


Example 2: Limiting to the First 5 Boosting Rounds
In this example, we restrict the prediction to use only the first 5 boosting rounds (trees) by setting iteration_range=(0, 5). This might be used for analyzing how early rounds affect predictions.
# Use the same trained model from Example 1
# Apply the model using only the first 5 trees
leaf_indices_partial = model.apply(X_test, iteration_range=(0, 5))


# Convert the results to a DataFrame (showing the first 10 samples)
df_ex2 = pd.DataFrame(leaf_indices_partial[:10],
                      columns=[f"Tree_{i}" for i in range(leaf_indices_partial.shape[1])])
df_ex2.insert(0, "Sample_Index", np.arange(10))
print("\nExample 2: Limiting to the First 5 Boosting Rounds")
print(df_ex2)


Simulated print() output:

Example 3: Extracting Leaf Indices for Multi-Class Classification
For multi-class classification (e.g., when using "multi:softprob"), each boosting round builds one tree per class. The apply() method will return a 2D array with leaf indices. Here, we demonstrate on a multi-class problem.
# Generate synthetic multi-class data
np.random.seed(202)
X_multi = np.random.rand(150, 5)
y_multi = np.random.randint(0, 3, size=150)  # 3 classes


# Create and train an XGBClassifier for multi-class classification
model_multi = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    n_estimators=10,
    max_depth=3,
    objective="multi:softprob",
    num_class=3,
    use_label_encoder=False,
    random_state=202
)
model_multi.fit(X_multi, y_multi, eval_metric="mlogloss")


# Apply the model to get leaf indices for all trees
leaf_indices_multi = model_multi.apply(X_multi)


# Create a DataFrame for the first 10 samples; note: the array shape depends on how XGBoost aggregates trees for multi-class
df_ex3 = pd.DataFrame(leaf_indices_multi[:10],
                      columns=[f"Tree_{i}" for i in range(leaf_indices_multi.shape[1])])
df_ex3.insert(0, "Sample_Index", np.arange(10))
print("\nExample 3: Extracting Leaf Indices for Multi-Class Classification")
print(df_ex3)


Simulated print() output:
Example 3: Extracting Leaf Indices for Multi-Class Classification
   Sample_Index  Tree_0  Tree_1  Tree_2  Tree_3  Tree_4  Tree_5  Tree_6  Tree_7  Tree_8  Tree_9
0             0       2       3       1       0       2       1       0       2       1       0
1             1       3       2       0       1       3       2       0       1       3       2
2             2       2       3       1       0       2       1       0       2       1       0
3             3       1       0       2       3       1       0       2       3       1       0
4             4       3       2       0       1       3       2       0       1       3       2
5             5       2       1       3       0       2       1       3       0       2       1
6             6       1       0       2       3       1       0       2       3       1       0
7             7       3       2       0       1       3       2       0       1       3       2
8             8       2       3       1       0       2       1       0       2       1       0
9             9       1       0       2       3       1       0       2       3       1       0


(In a multi-class setting, the actual structure may represent the concatenation of trees for each class; here we assume 10 columns corresponding to 10 boosting rounds.)
________________


Example 4: Using apply() for Feature Engineering
A common advanced use of apply() is to use the leaf indices as new features for a second-level model (e.g., linear regression). In this example, we obtain the leaf indices and then use them as input features for a simple linear regression model.
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split


# Generate synthetic regression data
np.random.seed(303)
X_reg = np.random.rand(100, 3)
y_reg = X_reg[:, 0] * 2.0 - X_reg[:, 1] * 1.0 + np.random.randn(100)*0.1


# Train an XGBRegressor (here we use gbtree for demonstration)
model_reg = xgb.XGBRegressor(n_estimators=10, max_depth=3, random_state=303)
model_reg.fit(X_reg, y_reg)


# Get leaf indices as new features
leaf_indices = model_reg.apply(X_reg)
# For simplicity, treat leaf indices as categorical features encoded as integers


# Use these leaf indices as features in a Linear Regression model
linreg = LinearRegression()
linreg.fit(leaf_indices, y_reg)
preds_linreg = linreg.predict(leaf_indices)


df_ex4 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Sample_Index": np.arange(10),
    "Original_Target": y_reg[:10],
    "LinearReg_Prediction": preds_linreg[:10]
})
print("\nExample 4: Using `apply()` for Feature Engineering")
print(df_ex4)


Simulated print() output:

   Sample_Index  Original_Target  LinearReg_Prediction
0             0         2.134500              2.101234
1             1         1.987654              1.965432
2             2         2.123456              2.101098
3             3         1.945678              1.923456
4             4         2.078901              2.056789

Example 5: Analyzing Prediction Stability Over Iterations
You might want to compare the leaf index predictions when using a subset of boosting rounds versus the full ensemble. In this example, we compare the outputs of apply() using all trees and using only the first 5 rounds.
# Use the model_reg from Example 1 or 4 for regression
leaf_indices_full = model_reg.apply(X_reg)
leaf_indices_partial = model_reg.apply(X_reg, iteration_range=(0, 5))


# Create DataFrames for the first 5 samples
df_full = pd.DataFrame(leaf_indices_full[:5],
                       columns=[f"Full_Tree_{i}" for i in range(leaf_indices_full.shape[1])])
df_partial = pd.DataFrame(leaf_indices_partial[:5],
                          columns=[f"Partial_Tree_{i}" for i in range(leaf_indices_partial.shape[1])])
df_full.insert(0, "Sample_Index", np.arange(5))
df_partial.insert(0, "Sample_Index", np.arange(5))


# Combine the two DataFrames side by side
df_ex5 = pd.concat([df_full, df_partial.iloc[:, 1:]], axis=1)
print("\nExample 5: Analyzing Prediction Stability Over Iterations")
print(df_ex5)


Simulated print() output:
Example 5: Analyzing Prediction Stability Over Iterations
   Sample_Index  Full_Tree_0  Full_Tree_1  Full_Tree_2  ...  Partial_Tree_2  Partial_Tree_3  Partial_Tree_4
0             0            2            3            1  ...               1               0               2
1             1            3            2            0  ...               0               1               3
2             2            2            3            1  ...               1               0               2
3             3            1            0            2  ...               2               3               1
4             4            3            2            0  ...               0               1               3


[5 rows x 15 columns]


(The output shows columns from the full prediction and columns from the partial prediction side by side for comparison.)

This is about Scikit-Learn API classxgboost.XGBClassifier Give me propertybest_iteration: int Full description Syntax Parameters description (in a non-table format) Return value give me 5 very detailed and good examples (make it long) give the result for each example with a pandas dataframe show me the print() output






Below is a comprehensive guide to the best_iteration property of the xgboost.XGBClassifier in the Scikit‑Learn API. This property is used to indicate the boosting round (iteration) at which the best performance was observed during training—typically when early stopping is enabled. It is useful for understanding how many rounds were effectively used by the model and can be used in subsequent prediction calls (for example, to restrict predictions to the best iteration).
________________


Full Description
The best_iteration property stores an integer value representing the 0-based index of the boosting round at which the best evaluation score was achieved during training when early stopping is enabled. In scenarios where early stopping is not used, it generally defaults to the final boosting round (often equal to n_estimators - 1). This property is automatically set after calling the .fit() method if you provide an evaluation set and specify early_stopping_rounds. It allows you to later use methods like .predict() with parameters such as iteration_range or ntree_limit to reproduce the best model output.
Key Points:
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           * Early Stopping:
 When early_stopping_rounds is provided during training, the model monitors the evaluation metric and stops if no improvement is seen for the specified number of rounds. The round with the best metric is stored in best_iteration.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           * Default Behavior:
 If early stopping is not used, best_iteration typically equals n_estimators - 1.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           * Usage:
 You can access this property to understand how many boosting rounds were effective. It is also useful in setting parameters for prediction methods that accept an iteration range.

________________


Syntax
best_iter = model.best_iteration


                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              * model: An instance of xgboost.XGBClassifier that has been fitted.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              * best_iter: An integer representing the best boosting round (0-based).

________________


Parameters Description (Non-Table Format)
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 * No parameters:
 The best_iteration property does not accept any arguments. It is a read-only attribute that is set during the call to .fit() when early stopping is used. If early stopping is not used, the property typically reflects the final iteration of the training process.

________________


Return Value
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    * Return Value:
 An integer that indicates the 0-based index of the boosting round at which the best evaluation performance was achieved. For example, if early stopping halted training at round 28 (meaning the 29th round was the best), best_iteration would be 28.

________________


5 Detailed Examples of best_iteration
Below are five comprehensive examples that demonstrate various use cases of the best_iteration property in xgboost.XGBClassifier. Each example includes code, an explanation, and a simulated pandas DataFrame summarizing the key outputs with a print output.
________________


Example 1: Basic Binary Classification with Early Stopping
In this example, we train a basic binary classifier using early stopping. After fitting, we retrieve the best_iteration property and display it in a DataFrame.
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split


# Generate synthetic binary classification data
np.random.seed(42)
X = np.random.rand(150, 4)
y = (X[:, 0] + X[:, 1] > 1).astype(int)


# Split into training and validation sets
X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.3, random_state=42)


# Create and train an XGBClassifier with early stopping
model = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    objective="binary:logistic",
    n_estimators=50,
    max_depth=3,
    use_label_encoder=False,
    random_state=42
)
model.fit(
    X_train,
    y_train,
    eval_set=[(X_val, y_val)],
    early_stopping_rounds=5,
    verbose=False,
    eval_metric="logloss"
)


# Access best_iteration property
best_iter = model.best_iteration


# Create a DataFrame summarizing the result
df_ex1 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Metric": ["Best Iteration"],
    "Value": [best_iter]
})
print("Example 1: Basic Binary Classification with Early Stopping")
print(df_ex1)


Simulated print() output:
Example 1: Basic Binary Classification with Early Stopping
           Metric  Value
0  Best Iteration     28


________________


Example 2: Classification Without Early Stopping
When early stopping is not used, best_iteration should reflect the final iteration (typically n_estimators - 1). This example trains a classifier without early stopping and then retrieves the best_iteration.
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split


# Generate synthetic data
np.random.seed(101)
X_cls = np.random.rand(100, 4)
y_cls = (X_cls[:, 0] + X_cls[:, 1] > 1).astype(int)


# Split data
X_train_cls, X_test_cls, y_train_cls, y_test_cls = train_test_split(X_cls, y_cls, test_size=0.3, random_state=101)


# Train XGBClassifier without early stopping
model_no_es = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    objective="binary:logistic",
    n_estimators=20,
    max_depth=3,
    use_label_encoder=False,
    random_state=101
)
model_no_es.fit(X_train_cls, y_train_cls, verbose=False)


# Retrieve best_iteration (should be 19 if 0-indexed)
best_iter_no_es = model_no_es.best_iteration


df_ex2 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Metric": ["Best Iteration (No Early Stopping)"],
    "Value": [best_iter_no_es]
})
print("\nExample 2: Classification Without Early Stopping")
print(df_ex2)


Simulated print() output:
Example 2: Classification Without Early Stopping
                                      Metric  Value
0  Best Iteration (No Early Stopping)     19


________________


Example 3: Multi-Class Classification with Early Stopping
In a multi-class scenario, the best_iteration property is still applicable. Here, we train an XGBClassifier for a 3-class problem with early stopping and then display the best iteration.
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split


# Generate synthetic multi-class data
np.random.seed(202)
X_multi = np.random.rand(200, 5)
y_multi = np.random.randint(0, 3, size=200)  # 3 classes


# Split data into training and validation sets
X_train_multi, X_val_multi, y_train_multi, y_val_multi = train_test_split(X_multi, y_multi, test_size=0.3, random_state=202)


# Create and train an XGBClassifier for multi-class classification
model_multi = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    objective="multi:softprob",
    num_class=3,
    n_estimators=50,
    max_depth=3,
    use_label_encoder=False,
    random_state=202
)
model_multi.fit(
    X_train_multi,
    y_train_multi,
    eval_set=[(X_val_multi, y_val_multi)],
    early_stopping_rounds=5,
    verbose=False,
    eval_metric="mlogloss"
)


best_iter_multi = model_multi.best_iteration


df_ex3 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Metric": ["Best Iteration (Multi-Class)"],
    "Value": [best_iter_multi]
})
print("\nExample 3: Multi-Class Classification with Early Stopping")
print(df_ex3)


Simulated print() output:
Example 3: Multi-Class Classification with Early Stopping
                              Metric  Value
0  Best Iteration (Multi-Class)     32


________________


Example 4: Using XGBClassifier in a Pipeline
When XGBClassifier is used within a scikit‑learn pipeline, you can still access the best_iteration property from the final step. In this example, we build a pipeline with preprocessing, train the model with early stopping, and then extract the best iteration.
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd


# Create a DataFrame with sample data
df_pipe = pd.DataFrame({
    "feature1": np.random.rand(100),
    "feature2": np.random.rand(100),
    "feature3": np.random.rand(100),
    "label": (np.random.rand(100) > 0.5).astype(int)
})
X_pipe = df_pipe[["feature1", "feature2", "feature3"]]
y_pipe = df_pipe["label"]


# Build a pipeline with StandardScaler and XGBClassifier
pipeline = Pipeline([
    ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
    ("xgb", xgb.XGBClassifier(
        objective="binary:logistic",
        n_estimators=30,
        max_depth=3,
        use_label_encoder=False,
        random_state=303
    ))
])
pipeline.fit(X_pipe, y_pipe, xgb__eval_set=[(X_pipe, y_pipe)], xgb__early_stopping_rounds=5, xgb__eval_metric="logloss")


# Retrieve best_iteration from the XGBClassifier step
best_iter_pipe = pipeline.named_steps["xgb"].best_iteration


df_ex4 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Metric": ["Best Iteration (Pipeline)"],
    "Value": [best_iter_pipe]
})
print("\nExample 4: Using XGBClassifier in a Pipeline")
print(df_ex4)


Simulated print() output:
Example 4: Using XGBClassifier in a Pipeline
                              Metric  Value
0      Best Iteration (Pipeline)     27


________________


Example 5: Comparing best_iteration Across Different Models
This example compares the best_iteration property of two models: one trained with early stopping and one trained without early stopping. We display the comparison in a DataFrame.
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split


# Generate synthetic binary classification data
np.random.seed(404)
X_comp = np.random.rand(150, 4)
y_comp = (X_comp[:, 0] + X_comp[:, 1] > 1).astype(int)


# Split the data
X_train_comp, X_val_comp, y_train_comp, y_val_comp = train_test_split(X_comp, y_comp, test_size=0.3, random_state=404)


# Model with early stopping
model_es = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    objective="binary:logistic",
    n_estimators=50,
    max_depth=3,
    use_label_encoder=False,
    random_state=404
)
model_es.fit(
    X_train_comp,
    y_train_comp,
    eval_set=[(X_val_comp, y_val_comp)],
    early_stopping_rounds=5,
    verbose=False,
    eval_metric="logloss"
)
best_iter_es = model_es.best_iteration


# Model without early stopping
model_no_es = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    objective="binary:logistic",
    n_estimators=50,
    max_depth=3,
    use_label_encoder=False,
    random_state=404
)
model_no_es.fit(X_train_comp, y_train_comp, verbose=False)
best_iter_no_es = model_no_es.best_iteration


df_ex5 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Model": ["With Early Stopping", "Without Early Stopping"],
    "Best_Iteration": [best_iter_es, best_iter_no_es]
})
print("\nExample 5: Comparing best_iteration Across Different Models")
print(df_ex5)


Simulated print() output:
Example 5: Comparing best_iteration Across Different Models
                    Model  Best_Iteration
0   With Early Stopping              31
1  Without Early Stopping            49


(The model without early stopping used all 50 rounds, so best_iteration is 49, whereas the early-stopping model stopped at round 31.)
________________


Summary

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       * Return Value:
An integer representing the 0-based index of the boosting round that achieved the best evaluation performance (when early stopping is used) or the final iteration index if early stopping was not used.
