Below is a comprehensive guide to the best_score property of the xgboost.XGBClassifier in the Scikit‑Learn API. This property stores the best evaluation metric score (typically a loss metric such as logloss for classification) observed during training when early stopping is used. It is a read‐only attribute that is set automatically during the call to .fit() if you provide an evaluation set along with the early_stopping_rounds parameter.
________________


Full Description
The best_score property reflects the optimal (best) value of the evaluation metric computed on the validation set during the training process. When you train an XGBoost classifier with early stopping enabled (by providing an eval_set and setting early_stopping_rounds), the training stops when the evaluation metric fails to improve for a specified number of rounds. At that point, the model stores:
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                * best_iteration: The boosting round (0-indexed) at which the best performance was observed.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                * best_score: The corresponding value of the evaluation metric at that round.

For example, if you use "logloss" as the evaluation metric for binary classification (with objective "binary:logistic"), then best_score will be the lowest logloss value observed on the evaluation set. If early stopping is not used, best_score typically reflects the evaluation metric computed at the final boosting round (or may remain undefined depending on version).
Key Points:
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   * Usage with Early Stopping:
 best_score is set only when early stopping is activated. It gives you insight into the model’s performance during training.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   * Interpretation:
 For classification, a lower best_score (e.g., logloss) is generally better.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   * Automatic Update:
 The property is automatically updated during .fit() when an evaluation set is provided.

________________


Syntax
best_score_value = model.best_score


                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      * model: A fitted instance of xgboost.XGBClassifier.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      * best_score_value: A float representing the best (optimal) evaluation metric value observed during training.

________________


Parameters Description (Non‑Table Format)
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         * No parameters:
 The best_score property does not take any arguments. It is a read-only attribute that you access after the model has been fitted with an evaluation set and early stopping.

________________


Return Value
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            * Return Value:
 A float representing the best evaluation score achieved during training. For example, if you are monitoring logloss, it might return a value like 0.4267 (with lower values indicating better performance). If early stopping is not used, best_score may simply reflect the evaluation metric computed at the final iteration.

________________


5 Detailed Examples of best_score
Below are five detailed examples that illustrate different scenarios in which the best_score property is used with xgboost.XGBClassifier. Each example includes a complete code snippet, an explanation, and a simulated pandas DataFrame summarizing key outputs with a print output.
________________


Example 1: Basic Binary Classification with Early Stopping
In this example, we generate synthetic binary classification data, train an XGBClassifier with early stopping, and then retrieve the best_score (the best logloss observed on the validation set).
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split


# Generate synthetic binary classification data
np.random.seed(42)
X = np.random.rand(150, 4)
y = (X[:, 0] + X[:, 1] > 1).astype(int)


# Split data into training and validation sets
X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.3, random_state=42)


# Create and train an XGBClassifier with early stopping
model = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    objective="binary:logistic",
    n_estimators=50,
    max_depth=3,
    use_label_encoder=False,
    random_state=42
)
model.fit(
    X_train,
    y_train,
    eval_set=[(X_val, y_val)],
    early_stopping_rounds=5,
    verbose=False,
    eval_metric="logloss"
)


# Retrieve best_score property
best_score_value = model.best_score


# Display the best score in a DataFrame
df_ex1 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Metric": ["Best Logloss Score"],
    "Value": [best_score_value]
})
print("Example 1: Basic Binary Classification with Early Stopping")
print(df_ex1)


Simulated print() output:
Example 1: Basic Binary Classification with Early Stopping
               Metric   Value
0  Best Logloss Score  0.4267


________________


Example 2: Classification Without Early Stopping
In this example, early stopping is not used. The best_score property then reflects the evaluation metric at the final boosting round. We train a classifier without early stopping and then retrieve best_score.
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split


# Generate synthetic data
np.random.seed(101)
X_cls = np.random.rand(100, 4)
y_cls = (X_cls[:, 0] + X_cls[:, 1] > 1).astype(int)


# Split data into training and test sets
X_train_cls, X_test_cls, y_train_cls, y_test_cls = train_test_split(X_cls, y_cls, test_size=0.3, random_state=101)


# Train XGBClassifier without early stopping
model_no_es = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    objective="binary:logistic",
    n_estimators=20,
    max_depth=3,
    use_label_encoder=False,
    random_state=101
)
model_no_es.fit(X_train_cls, y_train_cls, verbose=False)


# Retrieve best_score (expected to be from final iteration)
best_score_no_es = model_no_es.best_score


df_ex2 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Metric": ["Final Logloss Score (No Early Stopping)"],
    "Value": [best_score_no_es]
})
print("\nExample 2: Classification Without Early Stopping")
print(df_ex2)


Simulated print() output:
Example 2: Classification Without Early Stopping
                                        Metric   Value
0  Final Logloss Score (No Early Stopping)  0.5123


________________


Example 3: Multi-Class Classification with Early Stopping
Here, we train an XGBClassifier for a multi-class problem (3 classes) with early stopping enabled. The best_score will represent the best metric (e.g., multi-class logloss) achieved on the validation set.
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split


# Generate synthetic multi-class data
np.random.seed(202)
X_multi = np.random.rand(200, 5)
y_multi = np.random.randint(0, 3, size=200)


# Split the data into training and validation sets
X_train_multi, X_val_multi, y_train_multi, y_val_multi = train_test_split(X_multi, y_multi, test_size=0.3, random_state=202)


# Create and train an XGBClassifier for multi-class classification
model_multi = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    objective="multi:softprob",
    num_class=3,
    n_estimators=50,
    max_depth=3,
    use_label_encoder=False,
    random_state=202
)
model_multi.fit(
    X_train_multi,
    y_train_multi,
    eval_set=[(X_val_multi, y_val_multi)],
    early_stopping_rounds=5,
    verbose=False,
    eval_metric="mlogloss"
)


best_score_multi = model_multi.best_score


df_ex3 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Metric": ["Best Multi-Class Logloss Score"],
    "Value": [best_score_multi]
})
print("\nExample 3: Multi-Class Classification with Early Stopping")
print(df_ex3)


Simulated print() output:
Example 3: Multi-Class Classification with Early Stopping
                                  Metric   Value
0  Best Multi-Class Logloss Score  0.7356


________________


Example 4: Pipeline Integration with Early Stopping
When using XGBClassifier within a scikit‑learn pipeline, you can still access the best_score property from the final estimator. In this example, we build a pipeline with preprocessing, train the classifier with early stopping, and then extract the best score.
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd


# Create a DataFrame with sample data
df_pipe = pd.DataFrame({
    "f1": np.random.rand(100),
    "f2": np.random.rand(100),
    "f3": np.random.rand(100),
    "label": (np.random.rand(100) > 0.5).astype(int)
})
X_pipe = df_pipe[["f1", "f2", "f3"]]
y_pipe = df_pipe["label"]


# Build a pipeline with StandardScaler and XGBClassifier
pipeline = Pipeline([
    ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
    ("xgb", xgb.XGBClassifier(
        objective="binary:logistic",
        n_estimators=30,
        max_depth=3,
        use_label_encoder=False,
        random_state=303
    ))
])
# Fit the pipeline with early stopping (passing parameters to the xgb step)
pipeline.fit(X_pipe, y_pipe, xgb__eval_set=[(X_pipe, y_pipe)], xgb__early_stopping_rounds=5, xgb__eval_metric="logloss")


# Retrieve best_score from the XGBClassifier step
best_score_pipeline = pipeline.named_steps["xgb"].best_score


df_ex4 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Metric": ["Pipeline Best Logloss Score"],
    "Value": [best_score_pipeline]
})
print("\nExample 4: Pipeline Integration with Early Stopping")
print(df_ex4)


Simulated print() output:
Example 4: Pipeline Integration with Early Stopping
                             Metric   Value
0  Pipeline Best Logloss Score  0.4821


________________


Example 5: Comparing Best Score Across Different Random Seeds
In this example, we train two XGBClassifier models with different random seeds while using early stopping. We then compare their best_score values to understand how the random seed might affect early stopping outcomes.
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split


# Generate synthetic binary classification data
np.random.seed(505)
X_comp = np.random.rand(150, 4)
y_comp = (X_comp[:, 0] + X_comp[:, 1] > 1).astype(int)


# Split data into training and validation sets
X_train_comp, X_val_comp, y_train_comp, y_val_comp = train_test_split(X_comp, y_comp, test_size=0.3, random_state=505)


# Train model with random_state=505
model_seed1 = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    objective="binary:logistic",
    n_estimators=50,
    max_depth=3,
    use_label_encoder=False,
    random_state=505
)
model_seed1.fit(
    X_train_comp,
    y_train_comp,
    eval_set=[(X_val_comp, y_val_comp)],
    early_stopping_rounds=5,
    verbose=False,
    eval_metric="logloss"
)
best_score_seed1 = model_seed1.best_score


# Train model with random_state=606
model_seed2 = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    objective="binary:logistic",
    n_estimators=50,
    max_depth=3,
    use_label_encoder=False,
    random_state=606
)
model_seed2.fit(
    X_train_comp,
    y_train_comp,
    eval_set=[(X_val_comp, y_val_comp)],
    early_stopping_rounds=5,
    verbose=False,
    eval_metric="logloss"
)
best_score_seed2 = model_seed2.best_score


df_ex5 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Random_State": [505, 606],
    "Best_Logloss_Score": [best_score_seed1, best_score_seed2]
})
print("\nExample 5: Comparing Best Score Across Different Random Seeds")
print(df_ex5)


Simulated print() output:
Example 5: Comparing Best Score Across Different Random Seeds
   Random_State  Best_Logloss_Score
0           505              0.4892
1           606              0.5023

This is about Scikit-Learn API classxgboost.XGBClassifier Give me property coef_: ndarray Full description Syntax Parameters description (in a non-table format) Return value give me 5 very detailed and good examples (make it long) give the result for each example with a pandas dataframe show me the print() output










Below is a comprehensive guide to the coef_ property of the xgboost.XGBClassifier in the Scikit‑Learn API. Note that this property is only available when using the linear booster (i.e. when the classifier is instantiated with booster="gblinear"). In such cases, the classifier learns a linear model rather than an ensemble of trees, and the coef_ attribute stores the coefficients (weights) for each feature. For binary classification, the shape is typically (n_features,) (or sometimes (1, n_features)), and for multi-class classification, it is usually a 2D array with shape (n_classes, n_features).
________________


Full Description
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        * Purpose:
 The coef_ property provides the learned coefficient vector (or matrix) for a model that uses the linear booster (gblinear). These coefficients represent the contribution of each input feature to the prediction. They are analogous to the coefficients in classical linear models (such as LinearRegression or LogisticRegression) and can be used for interpretation, feature selection, or as a baseline for comparing with other linear models.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        * Availability:

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           * This property is only available if you instantiate your classifier with booster="gblinear".

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           * For tree-based boosters (the default is usually gbtree or dart), the model does not have a linear coefficient representation, and accessing coef_ will either result in an error or be undefined.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              * Usage:
 After fitting an XGBClassifier with booster="gblinear", you can inspect the coef_ property to see the learned weight for each feature. In multi-class problems (e.g., using objective="multi:softprob" with num_class specified), coef_ will be a matrix where each row corresponds to a class.

________________


Syntax
coefficients = model.coef_


Here, model is a fitted instance of xgboost.XGBClassifier (with booster="gblinear"), and coefficients is a NumPy ndarray.
________________


Parameters Description (Non‑Table Format)
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 * No parameters:
 The coef_ property is a read-only attribute; it does not accept any parameters. Its value is set automatically after the estimator has been fitted.

________________


Return Value
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    * Return Value:
 A NumPy ndarray containing the learned coefficients:

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       * For binary classification or regression (with a linear booster), the shape is typically (n_features,) (or sometimes (1, n_features)).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       * For multi-class classification, the shape is (n_classes, n_features), where each row corresponds to the coefficients for one class.

________________


5 Detailed Examples of coef_
Below are five detailed examples demonstrating various scenarios in which the coef_ property is used with an XGBClassifier configured with booster="gblinear". Each example includes complete code, an explanation, and a simulated pandas DataFrame summarizing the key outputs along with a simulated print output.
________________


Example 1: Basic Binary Classification with gblinear Booster
In this example, we generate synthetic binary classification data, fit an XGBClassifier with the linear booster, and then inspect the learned coefficient vector.
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split


# Generate synthetic binary classification data
np.random.seed(42)
X = np.random.rand(150, 4)
y = (X[:, 0] + X[:, 1] > 1).astype(int)


# Split data into training and testing sets
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.3, random_state=42)


# Instantiate and train XGBClassifier with gblinear booster
model_bin = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    objective="binary:logistic",
    booster="gblinear",
    n_estimators=10,
    random_state=42,
    use_label_encoder=False
)
model_bin.fit(X_train, y_train)


# Retrieve the coefficient vector
coef_bin = model_bin.coef_


# Create a DataFrame to display the coefficients
df_ex1 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Feature": [f"Feature_{i}" for i in range(coef_bin.shape[0])],
    "Coefficient": coef_bin
})
print("Example 1: Basic Binary Classification with gblinear Booster")
print(df_ex1)


Simulated print() output:
Example 1: Basic Binary Classification with gblinear Booster
     Feature  Coefficient
0  Feature_0     1.98765
1  Feature_1    -0.87654
2  Feature_2     0.54321
3  Feature_3     0.12345


________________


Example 2: Multi-Class Classification with gblinear Booster
Here, we train an XGBClassifier for a 3-class classification problem using the linear booster. The coef_ property returns a matrix where each row corresponds to a class.
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split


# Generate synthetic multi-class data
np.random.seed(101)
X_multi = np.random.rand(200, 5)
y_multi = np.random.randint(0, 3, size=200)  # 3 classes


# Split the data
X_train_multi, X_test_multi, y_train_multi, y_test_multi = train_test_split(X_multi, y_multi, test_size=0.3, random_state=101)


# Instantiate and train an XGBClassifier for multi-class classification
model_multi = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    objective="multi:softprob",
    num_class=3,
    booster="gblinear",
    n_estimators=15,
    random_state=101,
    use_label_encoder=False
)
model_multi.fit(X_train_multi, y_train_multi)


# Retrieve the coefficient matrix
coef_multi = model_multi.coef_


# Convert to a DataFrame for display
# Each row corresponds to a class; each column to a feature
df_ex2 = pd.DataFrame(coef_multi, columns=[f"Feature_{i}" for i in range(coef_multi.shape[1])])
df_ex2.insert(0, "Class", [f"Class_{i}" for i in range(coef_multi.shape[0])])
print("\nExample 2: Multi-Class Classification with gblinear Booster")
print(df_ex2)


Simulated print() output:
Example 2: Multi-Class Classification with gblinear Booster
      Class  Feature_0  Feature_1  Feature_2  Feature_3  Feature_4
0  Class_0     0.98765    -0.12345     0.45678     0.23456     0.34567
1  Class_1     1.23456    -0.23456     0.56789     0.34567     0.45678
2  Class_2     0.87654    -0.34567     0.67890     0.45678     0.56789


________________


Example 3: Using gblinear Booster in a Pipeline
In this example, we incorporate an XGBClassifier with booster="gblinear" into a scikit‑learn pipeline with preprocessing (StandardScaler). After fitting the pipeline, we extract the coefficients from the final step.
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd


# Create a DataFrame with sample data
df_pipe = pd.DataFrame({
    "f1": np.random.rand(100),
    "f2": np.random.rand(100),
    "f3": np.random.rand(100),
    "label": (np.random.rand(100) > 0.5).astype(int)
})
X_pipe = df_pipe[["f1", "f2", "f3"]]
y_pipe = df_pipe["label"]


# Build a pipeline with StandardScaler and XGBClassifier using gblinear booster
pipeline = Pipeline([
    ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
    ("xgb", xgb.XGBClassifier(
        objective="binary:logistic",
        booster="gblinear",
        n_estimators=10,
        random_state=42,
        use_label_encoder=False
    ))
])
pipeline.fit(X_pipe, y_pipe)


# Retrieve the coefficient vector from the XGBClassifier within the pipeline
coef_pipeline = pipeline.named_steps["xgb"].coef_


df_ex3 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Feature": [f"Feature_{i}" for i in range(coef_pipeline.shape[0])],
    "Coefficient": coef_pipeline
})
print("\nExample 3: Using gblinear Booster in a Pipeline")
print(df_ex3)


Simulated print() output:
Example 3: Using gblinear Booster in a Pipeline
     Feature  Coefficient
0  Feature_0     1.23456
1  Feature_1    -0.98765
2  Feature_2     0.45678


(Here, the scaler may change the scale of features, but the extracted coefficient vector still corresponds to the scaled features.)
________________


Example 4: Comparing Coefficients with Different Regularization Settings
Regularization parameters like L1 (alpha) and L2 (reg_lambda) affect the learned coefficients. In this example, we train two XGBClassifier models with booster="gblinear"—one with default regularization and one with stronger regularization—and then compare the coefficients.
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd


# Generate synthetic binary classification data
np.random.seed(303)
X_reg = np.random.rand(80, 3)
y_reg = (X_reg[:, 0] + X_reg[:, 1] > 1).astype(int)


# Model with default regularization
model_default = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    objective="binary:logistic",
    booster="gblinear",
    n_estimators=10,
    random_state=303,
    use_label_encoder=False
)
model_default.fit(X_reg, y_reg)
coef_default = model_default.coef_


# Model with strong regularization (higher alpha and reg_lambda)
model_strong_reg = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    objective="binary:logistic",
    booster="gblinear",
    n_estimators=10,
    alpha=1.0,         # L1 regularization
    reg_lambda=1.0,    # L2 regularization
    random_state=303,
    use_label_encoder=False
)
model_strong_reg.fit(X_reg, y_reg)
coef_strong = model_strong_reg.coef_


# Create a DataFrame comparing coefficients
df_ex4 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Feature": [f"Feature_{i}" for i in range(coef_default.shape[0])],
    "Coef_Default": coef_default,
    "Coef_StrongReg": coef_strong
})
print("\nExample 4: Comparing Coefficients with Different Regularization Settings")
print(df_ex4)


Simulated print() output:
Example 4: Comparing Coefficients with Different Regularization Settings
     Feature  Coef_Default  Coef_StrongReg
0  Feature_0       1.23456          0.87654
1  Feature_1      -0.98765         -0.54321
2  Feature_2       0.45678          0.23456


________________


Example 5: Inspecting Coefficients on a Small Dataset
This example demonstrates training an XGBClassifier with booster="gblinear" on a very small dataset to inspect the coefficient values in detail.
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd


# Generate a very small synthetic dataset
np.random.seed(404)
X_small = np.random.rand(10, 2)
y_small = (X_small[:, 0] + X_small[:, 1] > 1).astype(int)


# Train XGBClassifier with gblinear booster on the small dataset
model_small = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    objective="binary:logistic",
    booster="gblinear",
    n_estimators=5,
    random_state=404,
    use_label_encoder=False
)
model_small.fit(X_small, y_small)


# Retrieve the coefficient vector
coef_small = model_small.coef_


# Create a DataFrame to display coefficients for each feature
df_ex5 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Feature": [f"Feature_{i}" for i in range(coef_small.shape[0])],
    "Coefficient": coef_small
})
print("\nExample 5: Inspecting Coefficients on a Small Dataset")
print(df_ex5)


Simulated print() output:
Example 5: Inspecting Coefficients on a Small Dataset
     Feature  Coefficient
0  Feature_0       1.45678
1  Feature_1      -0.67890

                                                                                                                                                                    * Parameters:
 This is a read-only property that does not accept any parameters. It is available only for XGBoost classifiers (or regressors) that use the linear booster (i.e., booster="gblinear").

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   * Return Value:
 A NumPy ndarray containing the learned coefficients. For binary classification or regression, this is typically a 1D array of shape (n_features,) (or sometimes (1, n_features)). For multi-class classification, it is typically a 2D array with shape (n_classes, n_features), where each row corresponds to the coefficients for one class.
