This is about Scikit-Learn API classxgboost.XGBClassifier Give me evals_result() Full description Syntax Parameters description (in a non-table format) Return value give me 5 very detailed and good examples (make it long) give the result for each example with a pandas dataframe show me the print() output








Below is a comprehensive guide to the evals_result() method for the scikit‑learn API class xgboost.XGBClassifier. This method allows you to retrieve a detailed log of evaluation metrics computed during training when you specify one or more evaluation sets (via the eval_set parameter) in the call to .fit(). The resulting dictionary gives you insight into how the model’s performance evolved over boosting rounds.
________________


Full Description
When training an XGBClassifier with early stopping or simply with an evaluation set, you can pass an evaluation set (or sets) to the .fit() method. During training, the model computes specified evaluation metrics (such as logloss, error, or any custom metric) on each evaluation set at each boosting round. The evals_result() method then returns a nested dictionary that contains these metrics. The outer keys of the dictionary are strings corresponding to the names of the evaluation sets (by default, these are "validation_0", "validation_1", etc., with the training set sometimes included as well). For each evaluation set, there is an inner dictionary mapping metric names to a list of values—one value for each boosting round.
For example, if you train with eval_set=[(X_val, y_val)] and specify eval_metric="logloss", after training the call to evals_result() might return a dictionary such as:
{
  "validation_0": {
    "logloss": [0.678, 0.612, 0.587, 0.573, ...],
    "error":   [0.321, 0.290, 0.275, 0.265, ...]
  }
}


This detailed history is particularly useful for:
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   * Monitoring convergence: Visualizing metric trends to see if/when the model has converged.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   * Early stopping analysis: Understanding at which boosting round the performance stopped improving.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   * Hyperparameter tuning: Comparing evaluation histories for different parameter settings.

________________


Syntax
eval_history = model.evals_result()


                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      * model is an instance of xgboost.XGBClassifier that has been fitted with an eval_set.

________________


Parameters Description (Non‑Table Format)
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         * No parameters:
 The evals_result() method does not accept any parameters. It simply returns the evaluation history that was recorded during training. This history is only generated if you provided an evaluation set (using the eval_set parameter) when you called .fit(). If no evaluation set was provided, the returned dictionary may be empty or not contain evaluation metrics.

________________


Return Value
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            * Return Value:
 A dictionary where:

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               * The keys are strings representing the names of the evaluation sets. For example, "validation_0" corresponds to the first evaluation set passed to .fit().

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               * The values are nested dictionaries. In each nested dictionary, the keys are metric names (such as "logloss" or "error") and the values are lists of floats, with each float representing the metric value computed at that boosting round.

For example:

{
    "validation_0": {
        "logloss": [0.678, 0.612, 0.587, 0.573, ...],
        "error": [0.321, 0.290, 0.275, 0.265, ...]
    }
}
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  * ________________


5 Detailed Examples of evals_result()
Below are five examples that illustrate different scenarios in which evals_result() can be used with xgboost.XGBClassifier. Each example includes code, explanations, and simulated pandas DataFrames summarizing key outputs.
________________


Example 1: Basic Binary Classification with Early Stopping
In this example, we train an XGBClassifier on synthetic binary classification data using an evaluation set and early stopping. After training, we retrieve the evaluation history using evals_result() and display a subset of the logloss values in a DataFrame.
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split


# Generate synthetic binary classification data
np.random.seed(42)
X = np.random.rand(150, 4)
y = (X[:, 0] + X[:, 1] > 1).astype(int)


# Split data into training and validation sets
X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.3, random_state=42)


# Create and train an XGBClassifier with early stopping
model = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    objective="binary:logistic",
    n_estimators=50,
    max_depth=3,
    use_label_encoder=False,
    random_state=42
)
model.fit(
    X_train,
    y_train,
    eval_set=[(X_val, y_val)],
    early_stopping_rounds=5,
    verbose=False,
    eval_metric="logloss"
)


# Retrieve evaluation history
eval_history = model.evals_result()


# Create a DataFrame for the logloss values over iterations
df_ex1 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Iteration": np.arange(len(eval_history["validation_0"]["logloss"])),
    "Logloss": eval_history["validation_0"]["logloss"]
})
print("Example 1: Basic Binary Classification with Early Stopping")
print(df_ex1.head(10))


Simulated print() output:
Example 1: Basic Binary Classification with Early Stopping
   Iteration  Logloss
0          0   0.6789
1          1   0.6123
2          2   0.5874
3          3   0.5732
4          4   0.5610
5          5   0.5543
6          6   0.5501
7          7   0.5487
8          8   0.5479
9          9   0.5475


________________


Example 2: Evaluating Both Logloss and Error Metrics
This example shows how to record multiple metrics. We pass a list of evaluation metrics (["logloss", "error"]) during training and then display both in a combined DataFrame.
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split


# Generate synthetic binary classification data
np.random.seed(101)
X_cls = np.random.rand(150, 4)
y_cls = (X_cls[:, 0] + X_cls[:, 1] > 1).astype(int)


# Split data into training and validation sets
X_train_cls, X_val_cls, y_train_cls, y_val_cls = train_test_split(X_cls, y_cls, test_size=0.3, random_state=101)


# Train an XGBClassifier with two evaluation metrics
model_cls = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    objective="binary:logistic",
    n_estimators=40,
    max_depth=3,
    use_label_encoder=False,
    random_state=101
)
model_cls.fit(
    X_train_cls,
    y_train_cls,
    eval_set=[(X_val_cls, y_val_cls)],
    early_stopping_rounds=5,
    verbose=False,
    eval_metric=["logloss", "error"]
)


# Retrieve evaluation history
eval_history_cls = model_cls.evals_result()


# Create a DataFrame combining both metrics
iterations = np.arange(len(eval_history_cls["validation_0"]["logloss"]))
df_ex2 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Iteration": iterations,
    "Logloss": eval_history_cls["validation_0"]["logloss"],
    "Error": eval_history_cls["validation_0"]["error"]
})
print("\nExample 2: Evaluating Both Logloss and Error Metrics")
print(df_ex2.head(10))


Simulated print() output:

1          1   0.6423  0.2987

Example 3: Multi-Class Classification Evaluation History
In a multi-class setting (e.g., 3 classes), you can also record evaluation metrics. This example trains an XGBClassifier for multi-class classification and then displays the evaluation history (here, using mlogloss as the metric).
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split


# Generate synthetic multi-class data
np.random.seed(202)
X_multi = np.random.rand(200, 5)
y_multi = np.random.randint(0, 3, size=200)


# Split data into training and validation sets
X_train_multi, X_val_multi, y_train_multi, y_val_multi = train_test_split(X_multi, y_multi, test_size=0.3, random_state=202)


# Train an XGBClassifier for multi-class classification
model_multi = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    objective="multi:softprob",
    num_class=3,
    n_estimators=50,
    max_depth=3,
    use_label_encoder=False,
    random_state=202
)
model_multi.fit(
    X_train_multi,
    y_train_multi,
    eval_set=[(X_val_multi, y_val_multi)],
    early_stopping_rounds=5,
    verbose=False,
    eval_metric="mlogloss"
)


# Retrieve evaluation history
eval_history_multi = model_multi.evals_result()


# Create a DataFrame for the mlogloss metric
iterations_multi = np.arange(len(eval_history_multi["validation_0"]["mlogloss"]))
df_ex3 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Iteration": iterations_multi,
    "MultiClass_Logloss": eval_history_multi["validation_0"]["mlogloss"]
})
print("\nExample 3: Multi-Class Classification Evaluation History")
print(df_ex3.head(10))


Simulated print() output:
Example 3: Multi-Class Classification Evaluation History
   Iteration  MultiClass_Logloss
0          0             1.0987
1          1             1.0345
2          2             0.9789
3          3             0.9345
4          4             0.9012
5          5             0.8789
6          6             0.8623
7          7             0.8456
8          8             0.8301
9          9             0.8200

Example 4: Evaluating Model Performance in a Pipeline
When an XGBClassifier is part of a scikit‑learn pipeline, you can still retrieve the evaluation history from the XGBoost step. This example builds a pipeline with a standard scaler and then trains the classifier with an evaluation set. The evaluation history is then displayed.
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd


# Create synthetic data
np.random.seed(303)
df_pipe = pd.DataFrame({
    "f1": np.random.rand(100),
    "f2": np.random.rand(100),
    "f3": np.random.rand(100),
    "label": (np.random.rand(100) > 0.5).astype(int)
})
X_pipe = df_pipe[["f1", "f2", "f3"]]
y_pipe = df_pipe["label"]


# Build a pipeline
pipeline = Pipeline([
    ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
    ("xgb", xgb.XGBClassifier(
        objective="binary:logistic",
        n_estimators=30,
        max_depth=3,
        use_label_encoder=False,
        random_state=303
    ))
])
pipeline.fit(X_pipe, y_pipe, xgb__eval_set=[(X_pipe, y_pipe)], xgb__early_stopping_rounds=5, xgb__eval_metric="logloss")


# Retrieve evaluation history from the xgb step
eval_history_pipe = pipeline.named_steps["xgb"].evals_result()


iterations_pipe = np.arange(len(eval_history_pipe["validation_0"]["logloss"]))
df_ex4b = pd.DataFrame({
    "Iteration": iterations_pipe,
    "Logloss": eval_history_pipe["validation_0"]["logloss"]
})
print("\nExample 4: Evaluating Model Performance in a Pipeline")
print(df_ex4b.head(10))


Simulated print() output:
Example 4: Evaluating Model Performance in a Pipeline
   Iteration  Logloss
0          0   0.6856
1          1   0.6423
2          2   0.6012
3          3   0.5734
4          4   0.5567
5          5   0.5421
6          6   0.5345
7          7   0.5301
8          8   0.5289
9          9   0.5276


________________


Example 5: Detailed Analysis of Early Stopping Behavior
This example demonstrates how early stopping affects the evaluation history. We train a classifier with early stopping and then display a DataFrame showing both the number of iterations completed and the corresponding best evaluation metric (best score and best iteration) from the model properties.
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split


# Generate synthetic binary classification data
np.random.seed(404)
X_es = np.random.rand(150, 4)
y_es = (X_es[:, 0] + X_es[:, 1] > 1).astype(int)


# Split the data
X_train_es, X_val_es, y_train_es, y_val_es = train_test_split(X_es, y_es, test_size=0.3, random_state=404)


# Train the XGBClassifier with early stopping
model_es = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    objective="binary:logistic",
    n_estimators=50,
    max_depth=3,
    use_label_encoder=False,
    random_state=404
)
model_es.fit(
    X_train_es,
    y_train_es,
    eval_set=[(X_val_es, y_val_es)],
    early_stopping_rounds=5,
    verbose=False,
    eval_metric="logloss"
)


# Retrieve evaluation history and best iteration/score
eval_history_es = model_es.evals_result()
iterations_es = np.arange(len(eval_history_es["validation_0"]["logloss"]))
best_iter_es = model_es.best_iteration
best_score_es = model_es.best_score


df_ex5 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Iteration": iterations_es,
    "Logloss": eval_history_es["validation_0"]["logloss"]
})
df_summary = pd.DataFrame({
    "Best Iteration": [best_iter_es],
    "Best Logloss": [best_score_es]
})
print("\nExample 5: Detailed Analysis of Early Stopping Behavior")
print(df_ex5.head(10))
print("\nSummary:")
print(df_summary)


Simulated print() output:
Example 5: Detailed Analysis of Early Stopping Behavior
   Iteration  Logloss
0          0   0.6856
1          1   0.6423
2          2   0.6012
3          3   0.5734
4          4   0.5567
5          5   0.5421
6          6   0.5345
7          7   0.5301
8          8   0.5289
9          9   0.5276


Summary:
   Best Iteration  Best Logloss
0              31        0.4856

This is about Scikit-Learn API classxgboost.XGBClassifier Give me property feature_importances_: ndarray Full description Syntax Parameters description (in a non-table format) Return value give me 5 very detailed and good examples (make it long) give the result for each example with a pandas dataframe show me the print() output








Below is a comprehensive guide to the feature_importances_ property for the scikit‑learn API class xgboost.XGBClassifier. This property provides a NumPy array of importance scores for each feature used by the model, which is particularly meaningful when the model is using a tree‐based booster (typically "gbtree" or "dart"). The importance scores indicate how frequently (or how “useful”) each feature is in making splits across all the trees in the ensemble.
Note:
 The feature_importances_ property is available only when using tree-based boosters. If you use a linear booster (i.e. booster="gblinear"), you should use the coef_ property instead.
________________


Full Description
The feature_importances_ property in an XGBClassifier returns a 1D NumPy array where each element corresponds to the importance score of one input feature. These importance scores are generally computed as the "weight" — i.e., the number of times a feature is used to split the data across all trees. Some versions or settings of XGBoost may allow alternative importance types (such as "gain" or "cover"), but by default, the scikit‑learn API exposes the weight-based importance.
Key Points:
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    * Interpretation:
 Higher values indicate that a feature is more frequently used to make splits and is, therefore, considered more important for the prediction task.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    * Normalization:
 The raw importances may not sum to one; you might choose to normalize them for comparison or interpretation.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    * Usage:
 Feature importances can be used for model interpretation, feature selection, and understanding the underlying data relationships.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    * Availability:
 This property is computed after you call .fit() on the estimator and is available only when the model is using a tree-based booster.

________________


Syntax
import numpy as np


# After fitting an XGBClassifier, retrieve feature importances:
importance_array = model.feature_importances_


                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       * model is a fitted instance of xgboost.XGBClassifier.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       * importance_array is a NumPy array of shape (n_features,) containing the importance scores.

________________


Parameters Description (Non‑Table Format)
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          * No Parameters:
 The feature_importances_ property is read-only and does not accept any parameters. Its value is automatically set when the model is fitted.

________________


Return Value
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             * Return Value:
 A NumPy ndarray of shape (n_features,) where each element represents the importance of a feature. For a multi-class classifier using a tree-based booster, the importances are typically aggregated across all trees (i.e., across all classes) and returned as a single array.

________________


5 Detailed Examples of feature_importances_
Each example below demonstrates a different scenario using xgboost.XGBClassifier with a tree-based booster (the default booster is "gbtree"). In every example, we fit the model on synthetic data and then extract and display the feature importances in a pandas DataFrame.
________________


Example 1: Basic Binary Classification and Feature Importance Extraction
In this example, we generate synthetic binary classification data, train an XGBClassifier with default settings, and then extract the feature importances.
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split


# Generate synthetic binary classification data
np.random.seed(42)
X = np.random.rand(150, 4)
y = (X[:, 0] + X[:, 1] > 1).astype(int)


# Split data into training and testing sets
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.3, random_state=42)


# Create and train the XGBClassifier with default tree booster ("gbtree")
model = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    objective="binary:logistic",
    n_estimators=10,
    max_depth=3,
    use_label_encoder=False,
    random_state=42
)
model.fit(X_train, y_train)


# Retrieve feature importances
importances = model.feature_importances_


# Create a DataFrame summarizing the feature importances
df_ex1 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Feature": [f"Feature_{i}" for i in range(importances.shape[0])],
    "Importance": importances
})
print("Example 1: Basic Binary Classification and Feature Importance Extraction")
print(df_ex1)


Simulated print() output:
Example 1: Basic Binary Classification and Feature Importance Extraction
     Feature  Importance
0  Feature_0    0.412345
1  Feature_1    0.278901
2  Feature_2    0.189012
3  Feature_3    0.129742


________________


Example 2: Classification with Custom Booster Parameters
In this example, we update several parameters such as subsample and colsample_bytree before training an XGBClassifier and then extract the feature importances.
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd


# Generate synthetic binary classification data
np.random.seed(101)
X_cls = np.random.rand(150, 4)
y_cls = (X_cls[:, 0] + X_cls[:, 1] > 1).astype(int)


# Train an XGBClassifier with custom parameters
model_cls = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    objective="binary:logistic",
    n_estimators=15,
    max_depth=4,
    subsample=0.8,
    colsample_bytree=0.9,
    use_label_encoder=False,
    random_state=101
)
model_cls.fit(X_cls, y_cls, eval_metric="logloss")


# Retrieve feature importances
importances_cls = model_cls.feature_importances_


# Summarize in a DataFrame
df_ex2 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Feature": [f"Feature_{i}" for i in range(importances_cls.shape[0])],
    "Importance": importances_cls
})
print("\nExample 2: Classification with Custom Booster Parameters")
print(df_ex2)


Simulated print() output:
Example 2: Classification with Custom Booster Parameters
     Feature  Importance
0  Feature_0    0.501234
1  Feature_1    0.302345
2  Feature_2    0.125678
3  Feature_3    0.070743


________________


Example 3: Multi-Class Classification Feature Importances
For multi-class classification, even though the model builds separate trees per class, the feature_importances_ property aggregates the importance values into a single array. This example demonstrates that process.
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split


# Generate synthetic multi-class data
np.random.seed(202)
X_multi = np.random.rand(200, 5)
y_multi = np.random.randint(0, 3, size=200)  # 3 classes


# Split the data into training and validation sets
X_train_multi, X_test_multi, y_train_multi, y_test_multi = train_test_split(X_multi, y_multi, test_size=0.3, random_state=202)


# Create and train an XGBClassifier for multi-class classification
model_multi = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    objective="multi:softprob",
    num_class=3,
    n_estimators=20,
    max_depth=3,
    use_label_encoder=False,
    random_state=202
)
model_multi.fit(X_train_multi, y_train_multi, eval_metric="mlogloss")


# Retrieve aggregated feature importances
importances_multi = model_multi.feature_importances_


# Summarize in a DataFrame
df_ex3 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Feature": [f"Feature_{i}" for i in range(importances_multi.shape[0])],
    "Importance": importances_multi
})
print("\nExample 3: Multi-Class Classification Feature Importances")
print(df_ex3)


Simulated print() output:
Example 3: Multi-Class Classification Feature Importances
     Feature  Importance
0  Feature_0    0.420123
1  Feature_1    0.235678
2  Feature_2    0.198765
3  Feature_3    0.095432
4  Feature_4    0.049002


________________


Example 4: Using XGBClassifier in a Pipeline and Extracting Importances
This example demonstrates how to integrate XGBClassifier in a scikit‑learn pipeline (with preprocessing using StandardScaler) and then extract the feature importances from the final estimator.
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd


# Create a DataFrame with sample data
df_pipe = pd.DataFrame({
    "f1": np.random.rand(100),
    "f2": np.random.rand(100),
    "f3": np.random.rand(100),
    "f4": np.random.rand(100),
    "label": (np.random.rand(100) > 0.5).astype(int)
})
X_pipe = df_pipe[["f1", "f2", "f3", "f4"]]
y_pipe = df_pipe["label"]


# Build a pipeline with StandardScaler and XGBClassifier
pipeline = Pipeline([
    ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
    ("xgb", xgb.XGBClassifier(
        objective="binary:logistic",
        n_estimators=25,
        max_depth=4,
        use_label_encoder=False,
        random_state=303
    ))
])
pipeline.fit(X_pipe, y_pipe)


# Retrieve feature importances from the XGBClassifier step
importances_pipe = pipeline.named_steps["xgb"].feature_importances_


df_ex4 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Feature": [f"Feature_{i}" for i in range(importances_pipe.shape[0])],
    "Importance": importances_pipe
})
print("\nExample 4: Using XGBClassifier in a Pipeline and Extracting Importances")
print(df_ex4)


Simulated print() output:
Example 4: Using XGBClassifier in a Pipeline and Extracting Importances
     Feature  Importance
0  Feature_0    0.375432
1  Feature_1    0.275643
2  Feature_2    0.205678
3  Feature_3    0.143247


________________


Example 5: Comparing Feature Importances Across Different Hyperparameter Settings
This example trains two XGBClassifier models with different hyperparameter settings (e.g., varying max_depth) and compares their feature importances side by side.
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split


# Generate synthetic binary classification data
np.random.seed(505)
X_comp = np.random.rand(150, 4)
y_comp = (X_comp[:, 0] + X_comp[:, 1] > 1).astype(int)


# Split data into training and validation sets
X_train_comp, X_test_comp, y_train_comp, y_test_comp = train_test_split(X_comp, y_comp, test_size=0.3, random_state=505)


# Train model with max_depth=3
model_depth3 = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    objective="binary:logistic",
    n_estimators=30,
    max_depth=3,
    use_label_encoder=False,
    random_state=505
)
model_depth3.fit(X_train_comp, y_train_comp, eval_metric="logloss")
importances_depth3 = model_depth3.feature_importances_


# Train model with max_depth=5
model_depth5 = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    objective="binary:logistic",
    n_estimators=30,
    max_depth=5,
    use_label_encoder=False,
    random_state=505
)
model_depth5.fit(X_train_comp, y_train_comp, eval_metric="logloss")
importances_depth5 = model_depth5.feature_importances_


# Create a DataFrame comparing feature importances
df_ex5 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Feature": [f"Feature_{i}" for i in range(X_comp.shape[1])],
    "Importance_max_depth_3": importances_depth3,
    "Importance_max_depth_5": importances_depth5
})
print("\nExample 5: Comparing Feature Importances Across Different Hyperparameter Settings")
print(df_ex5)


Simulated print() output:
Example 5: Comparing Feature Importances Across Different Hyperparameter Settings
     Feature  Importance_max_depth_3  Importance_max_depth_5
0  Feature_0                0.412345                0.365432
1  Feature_1                0.278901                0.321098
2  Feature_2                0.189012                0.210987
3  Feature_3                0.129742                0.102483

                                                                                                                                    * Property: feature_importances_: ndarray

                                                                                                                                       * Return Value:
 A NumPy ndarray of shape (n_features,) containing the importance scores of the features. For tree-based classifiers (such as XGBClassifier with booster "gbtree" or "dart"), these scores typically represent the “weight” (i.e., the count of times a feature is used for splitting) and are often normalized or can be normalized manually.
