This is about Scikit-Learn API classxgboost.XGBClassifier Give me property feature_names_in_: ndarray Full description Syntax Parameters description (in a non-table format) Return value give me 5 very detailed and good examples (make it long) give the result for each example with a pandas dataframe show me the print() output










Below is a comprehensive guide to the feature_names_in_ property of the xgboost.XGBClassifier in the Scikit‑Learn API. This property records the feature names that were provided during training and is automatically set when the estimator’s .fit() method is called with an input that contains column names (typically, a pandas DataFrame). It helps ensure that during prediction the features are aligned with the training data.
________________


Full Description
The feature_names_in_ property is part of the scikit‑learn estimator API and is now supported by many estimators, including XGBoost’s XGBClassifier. When you train an XGBoost model using a pandas DataFrame (or any object with feature names), the estimator stores the names of the features used during training. This is useful for:
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         * Validation: Ensuring that the input provided at prediction time has the same features as during training.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         * Interpretability: Allowing you to know which features were used, especially in complex pipelines.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         * Debugging: Helping to catch errors if the features’ order or names differ from what the model expects.

If the model is trained on data without explicit feature names (e.g., a NumPy array), then feature_names_in_ may be set to default names (often strings of indices, e.g. ['0', '1', '2', …]).
________________


Syntax
feature_names = model.feature_names_in_


                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            * model is an instance of xgboost.XGBClassifier that has been fitted using .fit(X, y) where X includes feature names.

________________


Parameters Description (Non‑Table Format)
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               * No parameters:
 The feature_names_in_ property is a read-only attribute that does not accept any parameters. Its value is determined at the time of fitting by inspecting the input data provided to .fit().

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  * If you pass a pandas DataFrame with column names, then feature_names_in_ will be a NumPy array containing those column names.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  * If you pass an array-like object without feature names (for example, a NumPy array), then feature_names_in_ will typically be set to default names (such as strings representing the feature indices).

________________


Return Value
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     * Return Value:
 A NumPy ndarray of shape (n_features,) containing the feature names as strings.

For example, if you trained your model on a DataFrame with columns ["age", "income", "score"], then after fitting, model.feature_names_in_ would return:

np.array(["age", "income", "score"])
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        *                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         * If no explicit feature names were provided, you might see default names like ["0", "1", "2"].

________________


5 Detailed Examples of feature_names_in_
Below are five examples demonstrating various use cases of the feature_names_in_ property in an XGBClassifier. Each example includes code, explanation, and simulated print output with a pandas DataFrame summarizing key results.
________________


Example 1: Basic Usage with a Pandas DataFrame
In this example, we create a synthetic binary classification dataset as a pandas DataFrame. We then train an XGBClassifier and inspect the feature_names_in_ property.
import xgboost as xgb
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split


# Create a DataFrame with named columns
df = pd.DataFrame({
    "age": np.random.randint(18, 70, size=150),
    "income": np.random.randint(30000, 100000, size=150),
    "score": np.random.rand(150)
})
# Create binary target (for simplicity, use threshold on 'score')
df["label"] = (df["score"] > 0.5).astype(int)


# Split features and target
X = df[["age", "income", "score"]]
y = df["label"]


# Split into training and test sets
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.3, random_state=42)


# Instantiate and fit the classifier
model = xgb.XGBClassifier(objective="binary:logistic", n_estimators=10, use_label_encoder=False, random_state=42)
model.fit(X_train, y_train, eval_metric="logloss")


# Retrieve feature names
feature_names = model.feature_names_in_


# Create a DataFrame for display
df_ex1 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Feature_Index": np.arange(len(feature_names)),
    "Feature_Name": feature_names
})
print("Example 1: Basic Usage with a Pandas DataFrame")
print(df_ex1)


Simulated print() output:
Example 1: Basic Usage with a Pandas DataFrame
   Feature_Index Feature_Name
0              0          age
1              1       income
2              2        score


________________


Example 2: Training on a NumPy Array (Default Feature Names)
In this example, we train the model on a NumPy array instead of a DataFrame. In this case, no custom feature names are provided, so the feature_names_in_ property should contain default names.
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split


# Generate synthetic data as NumPy arrays
np.random.seed(101)
X_np = np.random.rand(150, 4)
y_np = (X_np[:, 0] + X_np[:, 1] > 1).astype(int)


# Split the data
X_train_np, X_test_np, y_train_np, y_test_np = train_test_split(X_np, y_np, test_size=0.3, random_state=101)


# Instantiate and fit the classifier on NumPy arrays
model_np = xgb.XGBClassifier(objective="binary:logistic", n_estimators=15, use_label_encoder=False, random_state=101)
model_np.fit(X_train_np, y_train_np, eval_metric="logloss")


# Retrieve feature names (likely default names)
feature_names_np = model_np.feature_names_in_


df_ex2 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Feature_Index": np.arange(len(feature_names_np)),
    "Feature_Name": feature_names_np
})
print("\nExample 2: Training on a NumPy Array (Default Feature Names)")
print(df_ex2)


Simulated print() output:
Example 2: Training on a NumPy Array (Default Feature Names)
   Feature_Index Feature_Name
0              0            0
1              1            1
2              2            2
3              3            3


________________


Example 3: Using a Column Transformer in a Pipeline
In this example, an XGBClassifier is used as the final estimator in a pipeline that includes a ColumnTransformer. Only selected columns are passed to the classifier, so feature_names_in_ should reflect those selected columns.
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
import xgboost as xgb
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np


# Create a sample DataFrame with more features
df_multi = pd.DataFrame({
    "age": np.random.randint(18, 70, size=150),
    "income": np.random.randint(30000, 100000, size=150),
    "score": np.random.rand(150),
    "experience": np.random.randint(0, 40, size=150)
})
df_multi["label"] = (df_multi["income"] > 50000).astype(int)


# Define a ColumnTransformer to select only specific columns
ct = ColumnTransformer([
    ("select", "passthrough", ["age", "score"])
])


# Build a pipeline with the transformer and XGBClassifier
pipeline = Pipeline([
    ("transformer", ct),
    ("classifier", xgb.XGBClassifier(objective="binary:logistic", n_estimators=10, use_label_encoder=False, random_state=42))
])
X_multi_features = df_multi.drop("label", axis=1)
y_multi_label = df_multi["label"]
pipeline.fit(X_multi_features, y_multi_label)


# Retrieve feature names from the final classifier
feature_names_pipe = pipeline.named_steps["classifier"].feature_names_in_


df_ex3 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Feature_Index": np.arange(len(feature_names_pipe)),
    "Feature_Name": feature_names_pipe
})
print("\nExample 3: Using a Column Transformer in a Pipeline")
print(df_ex3)


Simulated print() output:
Example 3: Using a Column Transformer in a Pipeline
   Feature_Index Feature_Name
0              0          age
1              1        score


________________


Example 4: Multi-Class Classification with a DataFrame
For multi-class classification, we train an XGBClassifier using a pandas DataFrame. The feature_names_in_ property should reflect the column names of the training DataFrame.
import xgboost as xgb
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split


# Create a DataFrame with named features for multi-class classification
df_mc = pd.DataFrame({
    "f1": np.random.rand(200),
    "f2": np.random.rand(200),
    "f3": np.random.rand(200),
    "f4": np.random.rand(200)
})
df_mc["label"] = np.random.randint(0, 3, size=200)  # 3 classes


# Split the data
X_mc = df_mc[["f1", "f2", "f3", "f4"]]
y_mc = df_mc["label"]
X_train_mc, X_test_mc, y_train_mc, y_test_mc = train_test_split(X_mc, y_mc, test_size=0.3, random_state=202)


# Train an XGBClassifier for multi-class classification
model_mc = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    objective="multi:softprob",
    num_class=3,
    n_estimators=20,
    max_depth=3,
    use_label_encoder=False,
    random_state=202
)
model_mc.fit(X_train_mc, y_train_mc, eval_metric="mlogloss")


# Retrieve feature names
feature_names_mc = model_mc.feature_names_in_


df_ex4 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Feature_Index": np.arange(len(feature_names_mc)),
    "Feature_Name": feature_names_mc
})
print("\nExample 4: Multi-Class Classification with a DataFrame")
print(df_ex4)


Simulated print() output:
Example 4: Multi-Class Classification with a DataFrame
   Feature_Index Feature_Name
0              0          f1
1              1          f2
2              2          f3
3              3          f4


________________


Example 5: Changing Input Format and Observing Feature Names
In this example, we simulate the situation where a user converts a DataFrame to a NumPy array before training. In that case, the model will not receive the original column names and will default to generic feature names. We then examine the feature_names_in_ property.
import xgboost as xgb
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np


# Create a DataFrame with column names
df_input = pd.DataFrame({
    "A": np.random.rand(100),
    "B": np.random.rand(100),
    "C": np.random.rand(100)
})
y_input = (df_input["A"] + df_input["B"] > 1).astype(int)


# Convert the DataFrame to a NumPy array (losing column names)
X_np = df_input.values


# Train an XGBClassifier on the NumPy array
model_np = xgb.XGBClassifier(n_estimators=10, use_label_encoder=False, random_state=101)
model_np.fit(X_np, y_input, eval_metric="logloss")


# Retrieve feature names (likely defaults since input had no names)
feature_names_np = model_np.feature_names_in_


df_ex5 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Feature_Index": np.arange(len(feature_names_np)),
    "Feature_Name": feature_names_np
})
print("\nExample 5: Changing Input Format and Observing Feature Names")
print(df_ex5)


Simulated print() output:
Example 5: Changing Input Format and Observing Feature Names
   Feature_Index Feature_Name
0              0           0
1              1           1
2              2           2


________________

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          * Parameters:
 This is a read-only property that does not take parameters. It is automatically set when the estimator is fitted using input data that contains feature names.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              * Return Value:
 A NumPy ndarray of shape (n_features,) containing the names of the features used during training.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 * If the model is trained on a pandas DataFrame, the array contains the column names.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 * If the model is trained on an array-like structure without names, default names (e.g., strings of indices) are provided.

The five detailed examples demonstrate various real‑world use cases:
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    1. Basic Usage with a DataFrame: Training on a DataFrame and retrieving the feature names.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   2. Training on a NumPy Array: Showing default feature names when no column names are provided.
